title: runner-pi: add baseline coverage before auto-routing promotion
description: pi is a registered supported subprocess runner but should remain explicit-only until it has full baseline coverage comparable to primary runneres. Add or audit coverage for argument construction, prompt/workdir delivery, route-target selection/listing, reasoning/thinking support, token usage, error/cancel behavior, permission semantics, final metadata, and live or recorded evidence. Only promote pi to auto-routing eligibility after the evidence exists.\n\nIn-scope files are expected to include internal/runneres/pi/, service/routing tests, capability metadata, and docs evidence.
acceptance:
1. pi runner tests cover route-target flag, thinking/reasoning flag, workdir or subprocess Dir behavior, prompt delivery, timeout/cancellation cleanup, stderr/exit mapping, final text, and usage parsing if supported.
2. Route-target listing and reasoning/thinking support are implemented with source metadata or explicitly declared unsupported with routing tests.
3. Permission semantics are documented and tested.
4. Capability matrix/docs include evidence pointers and blockers.
5. pi is promoted to auto-routing eligibility only if AC1-AC4 are satisfied; otherwise it remains explicit-only.
6. go test ./... passes.

title: service: implement DdxAgent.ListRunneres with rich RunnerInfo (install, perms, efforts, quota)
description:
Implement DdxAgent.ListRunneres(ctx) per CONTRACT-001. Returns []RunnerInfo where each entry includes:
- Name, Type ("native" | "subprocess")
- Available, Path, Error
- IsLocal, IsSubscription, ExactPinSupport
- SupportedPermissions (subset of safe/supervised/unrestricted) — derived from PermissionArgs map keys
- SupportedEfforts (subset of low/medium/high) — derived from ReasoningLevels
- CostClass (local | cheap | medium | expensive)
- Quota (live; nil if not applicable; for claude/codex this consults the cache + tmux probe)

Backed by the registry+state ports (agent-X for registry+state). Per-runner Quota field populated from the runner implementation when available.
acceptance:
- [ ] DdxAgent.ListRunneres(ctx) returns one RunnerInfo per registered runner
- [ ] SupportedPermissions and SupportedEfforts are accurate per runner (claude has all 3 of each; gemini has different subsets)
- [ ] Quota field is non-nil for claude/codex when their tmux probe succeeds
- [ ] Test exercises at least claude + 1 other runner

title: BLOCKER FIXED: tests leak GIT_DIR into parent repo .git/config
description: Lefthook sets GIT_DIR during pre-commit and Go tests inherit it when spawning git subprocesses, mutating the parent repo config with stray 'worktree = /tmp/TestXxx/NNN' lines. Fixed in commit 2032819 by adding TestMain GIT_* prefix scrub to internal/git, internal/server, internal/agent, internal/bead, and cmd test packages. This was the root cause of all the .git/config corruption during concurrent execute-bead runs.
acceptance: FIXED in commit 2032819 (main). Integration test needed: run execute-bead from inside a linked worktree and assert shared .git/config is unchanged after completion.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — execution-ready bead authoring
description:
Create skills/ddx/reference/beads.md — the domain reference file a runner reads when the user asks to create, plan, or update beads.

Content required:
- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.
- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.
- **Explicit do-NOT-touch scope negation** (non-goals). Prevents scope creep during agent execution.
- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.
- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli && go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.
- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.
- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.
- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.
- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level "Execution-Ready" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)
- skills/ddx/reference/{work,review,agents,status}.md (separate beads)
acceptance: File exists at skills/ddx/reference/beads.md. Contains sections covering: inline context requirement, explicit file scope (in + out), small-bead rule of thumb, command-based acceptance criteria, tests-included requirement, rollback expectations, spec-id guidance, CLI reference. No occurrences of 'phase:(frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review)' in body. No mandatory 'helix' label requirement (grep -E 'helix.*(required|must|required)' returns no matches). Linked from skills/ddx/SKILL.md intent-router.

title: integration suite: cost cap + determinism controls + parity comparison reporting
description:
Add safety + reproducibility controls to the integration suite (depends on agent-X scaffolding bead).

- --max-cost-usd <N> flag (default $0.50). Halts the sweep when accumulated cost exceeds cap. Per-task estimate checked against remaining budget pre-invocation; over-budget tasks skip with explicit "skipped: cost cap" reason.
- temperature=0 + per-task seed for runneres/upstream-services that support deterministic sampling. Seeds recorded alongside results so reruns are reproducible.
- Non-deterministic runneres (no seed support) flagged in the report ("non-deterministic: {reason}"); their parity-comparison results treated as advisory only.
- Parity comparison method: tool-call sequence equality is primary signal — same sequence of (tool_name, normalized_args) = behaviorally equivalent. String-similarity (cosine on character n-grams) as secondary signal flagging "looks similar but not identical." NO automated semantic equivalence — humans review divergences.
- Future scope (out, file follow-up beads): embedding-similarity, LLM-as-judge.
acceptance:
- [ ] --max-cost-usd cap halts sweep when exceeded; per-task pre-check works
- [ ] temperature=0 + seed set on every supporting runner call; recorded in results
- [ ] Non-deterministic runneres flagged in report
- [ ] Parity report tabulates tool-call sequences and final-string-similarity for human review

title: bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded
description:
Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).

## Two-part bug

### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)

Initial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.

We assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No route-targets loaded' error instead of 'unknown route-target'.

Verified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, route-target loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.

### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice

The routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare route-target names against an LM Studio upstream-service. The standard discovery flow should be:

1. GET /v1/route-targets → enumerate the catalog the server is offering
2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)
3. Cache the (upstream-service, route-target_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL
4. When user code asks for 'route-target qwen3-coder-next' against this upstream-service, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list

This converts the 'No route-targets loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested route-target qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).

## Impact on cost-tiered routing

The cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable upstream-service readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:
- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation
- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input
- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier

## Fix scope

- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID
- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)
- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)
- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)
acceptance: (1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. (2) Bare route-target names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) upstream-services command shows http-connected and inference-ready-route-targets per upstream-service. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-route-targets, not 'connected (N route-targets)'. (5) Regression test uses a mock LM Studio that lists 'qwen/foo' but rejects 'foo' on chat/completions; discovery normalizes 'foo' → 'qwen/foo' and the probe succeeds.

bead.title: bug: /v1/route-targets is not a liveness probe — upstream-services shows 'connected' when LM Studio has no route-target loaded
bead.description:
Discovered 2026-04-15 while routing work to vidar-coder (LM Studio on vidar:1234).

## Two-part bug

### Part 1: bare-name JIT miss (the actual root cause)

Initial probe: `POST /v1/chat/completions {route-target: 'qwen3-coder-next', ...}` → 400 'No route-targets loaded'.

We assumed LM Studio had no route-target loaded. Wrong. The actual route-target is listed by /v1/route-targets as 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (with the 'qwen/' prefix). LM Studio's just-in-time loading IS enabled and DOES auto-load route-targets on demand — but only when the requested route-target ID matches one in /v1/route-targets exactly. Bare-name 'qwen3-coder-next' didn't match, so JIT couldn't fire, and LM Studio returned the misleading 'No route-targets loaded' error instead of 'unknown route-target'.

Verified by re-probing with 'qwen/qwen3-coder-next' — JIT fired, route-target loaded, responded 'OK' in ~60s on cold start.

### Part 2: discovery should be standard practice

The routing/probe layer should never trust user-supplied bare route-target names against an LM Studio upstream-service. The standard discovery flow should be:

1. GET /v1/route-targets → enumerate the catalog the server is offering
2. For each catalog ID, optionally probe POST /v1/chat/completions with a 1-token request and a generous timeout (60s+ on cold start, 3s on warm)
3. Cache the (upstream-service, route-target_id) → inference-ready bool with a short TTL
4. When user code asks for 'route-target qwen3-coder-next' against this upstream-service, normalize against the discovered catalog: if there's exactly one match by suffix, accept it; if zero or multiple, error with the candidate list

This converts the 'No route-targets loaded' misleading error into a clear 'requested route-target qwen3-coder-next not in catalog; closest match: qwen/qwen3-coder-next' (or just transparent normalization).

## Impact on cost-tiered routing

The cost-tiered routing policy (DDx bead ddx-dc231144 tier-based auto-escalation) needs reliable upstream-service readiness signals. Without discovery + normalization:
- bare-name configs silently fail and force escalation
- the 'connected (N route-targets)' status is unreliable as a routing input
- every misrouted attempt burns latency and forces the next tier

## Fix scope

- Update routing/health probe in cmd/ddx-agent/routing.go (or equivalent) to enumerate /v1/route-targets and probe each listed route-target with its exact ID
- Add a route-target-id normalization layer that maps bare names to catalog IDs by suffix-match (with explicit error on ambiguity)
- Cache probe results per (upstream-service, route-target) for a short TTL (30s reasonable)
- Surface the distinction in 'ddx-agent upstream-services' output: http-connected (from /v1/route-targets) vs inference-ready-route-targets (the subset that actually responds)
bead.acceptance: (1) GET /v1/route-targets is enumerated by the discovery layer; the catalog is the canonical route-target-ID source per upstream-service. (2) Bare route-target names ('qwen3-coder-next') passed by callers are normalized to canonical IDs ('qwen/qwen3-coder-next') by suffix-match against the catalog, with explicit ambiguity errors. (3) upstream-services command shows http-connected and inference-ready-route-targets per upstream-service. (4) Routing policy consumes inference-ready-route-targets, not 'connected (N route-targets)'. description:

Integrate normalized routing signals into selection and operator-facing surfaces.

## Scope
- Feed routing candidate planning from normalized signals
- Update doctor routing output with source and freshness details
- Rework `ddx agent usage` to consume adapters/metrics instead of a duplicate session ledger
- Use quota snapshot history and relative subscription-burn estimation as balancing inputs where applicable
- Consume the freshest cached quota snapshot rather than inline PTY scraping when live probing is required
- Add routing tests for quota blocked vs unknown behavior
acceptance: Routing, `ddx agent doctor --routing`, and `ddx agent usage` all consume the normalized routing signal route-target; quota blocked candidates are rejected, unknown quota remains viable with reduced confidence, and selection explanations include source freshness, subscription-burn inputs, and the freshest cached snapshot when live probing is involved

title: correctness: define mixed-provenance root cost rollup semantics
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-otel-telemetry-capture.md:221
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: CONTRACT-001 says the root invoke_agent span should emit a session cost rollup whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only become unknown when any child cost is unknown. But the contract never defines what ddx.cost.source, ddx.cost.currency, and ddx.cost.pricing_ref should look like when all child chat costs are known yet their provenance differs across turns, such as one upstream-service-reported turn plus one configured-cost turn. That makes the required root shape ambiguous: ddx.cost.source is required when amount is known, but the allowed enum only route-targets single-attempt provenance. The current implementation fell into this gap and downgraded a known total to unknown when provenance metadata differed.
Suggested fix: Amend the root-span cost-rollup section to specify a conformant representation for mixed known-cost provenance. Either add an explicit aggregate/root rollup source shape, or state that the root may still emit ddx.cost.amount and currency while omitting or summarizing provenance fields that cannot be represented cleanly. Add a contract-focused regression test or checklist entry covering mixed upstream-service-reported/configured sessions.
acceptance: CONTRACT-001 explicitly defines how the root invoke_agent span represents known session totals when child chat spans have mixed known provenance; the contract no longer requires an impossible or ambiguous ddx.cost.source/pricing_ref combination at the root; conformance tests or checklist coverage include a mixed upstream-service-reported/configured session example.

title: cache: cost attribution at the native loop using already-existing manifest fields
description:
Bead D of the prompt-caching plan (codex-refined). Wire the cache-token costs that Anthropic and OpenAI already report into core.CostAttribution. Today the configured-cost fallback at internal/core/loop.go:303 prices only Input + Output; cache-read tokens are counted at full input price for cost attribution even though Anthropic/OpenAI charge ~10% / ~50% of base. Depends on bead A (agent-cccc2df7) for the CachePolicy="off" semantics.

Codex correctly diagnosed the wiring: manifest v4 already has CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM at internal/route-targetcatalog/manifest.go:44-45 (route-target entries) and :139-140 (target entries) with validation. CostAttribution already has CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount at internal/core/agent.go:198-199. Telemetry emission already wires them at internal/core/loop.go:936-940. The only missing piece is: PricingFor at internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 drops the cache fields when projecting to core.Route-targetPricing, and core.Route-targetPricing at internal/core/pricing.go:5 lacks the slots, so loop.go:303 has no source-of-truth to read.

Three coordinated edits:

1. internal/core/pricing.go:5 — add CacheReadPerM, CacheWritePerM float64 to core.Route-targetPricing.

2. internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go:388 (PricingFor) — preserve CostCacheReadPerM and CostCacheWritePerM from the manifest route-target/target onto the returned Route-targetPricing.

3. internal/core/loop.go:303 — extend the configured-cost computation to add cache-read and cache-write costs:
   computed := input/1e6 * InputPerMTok + output/1e6 * OutputPerMTok
            + cacheRead/1e6 * CacheReadPerM
            + cacheWrite/1e6 * CacheWritePerM
   Source the cache token counts from resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens and CacheWriteTokens. Populate CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = &cacheReadCost and CacheWriteAmount = &cacheWriteCost (slots already there).

4. Honor CachePolicy="off" from bead A: when off, emit CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount as explicit *float64(0.0), NOT nil. nil = runner/upstream-service didn't report; explicit zero = caller asked for no caching. This matches the existing zero-vs-unknown convention from agent-a8cbdb87.

5. Update CONTRACT-003 prose: paragraph in the Cost Attribution / UsageReport section explaining that cache_read and cache_write tokens are priced at the manifest's CostCacheReadPerM/CostCacheWritePerM rates; nil cache amounts mean the runner or upstream-service didn't report cache usage; explicit zero means caching was opted out via CachePolicy="off".

Historical session-log recalculation is OUT OF SCOPE per codex: existing logs cannot be re-priced reliably from UsageReport alone without changing semantics. New runs price correctly going forward; old logs keep their existing numbers.

In-scope files:
- internal/core/pricing.go (struct fields)
- internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go (PricingFor preservation)
- internal/core/loop.go (cost computation at line 303)
- internal/core/loop_test.go (or new file: cache-cost test cases)
- internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog_test.go (PricingFor preservation test)
- docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md (cost-semantics prose)

Out of scope:
- Anthropic cache_control writes (bead C)
- OpenAI prefix-stability test (bead B)
- UsageReport historical recalculation
- New CHANGELOG entry beyond what the bead's commit message produces
acceptance:
1. core.Route-targetPricing has CacheReadPerM and CacheWritePerM float64 fields. AST-checked: TestRoute-targetPricingHasCacheFields.
2. PricingFor preserves cache fields: TestPricingForPreservesCacheFieldsFromManifest loads a manifest fixture with non-zero cost_cache_read_per_m / cost_cache_write_per_m on a route-target entry, calls PricingFor, asserts the returned Route-targetPricing carries those values verbatim.
3. Native loop populates cache cost: TestNativeLoopPopulatesCacheCostAttribution drives a fixture catalog through a virtual upstream-service that emits resp.Usage.CacheReadTokens=1000 and CacheWriteTokens=500. Asserts CostAttribution.CacheReadAmount = (1000 / 1e6) * CostCacheReadPerM and CacheWriteAmount = (500 / 1e6) * CostCacheWritePerM, both as non-nil *float64.
4. CachePolicy=off semantics: TestCachePolicyOffZerosCacheAttribution drives the same loop with ServiceExecuteRequest.CachePolicy="off"; asserts CacheReadAmount and CacheWriteAmount are non-nil *float64 with value 0.0 (NOT nil).
5. CONTRACT-003 has the cost-semantics prose paragraph; doc-link grep test asserts the prose mentions "cost_cache_read_per_m", "explicit zero", and "opted out".
6. go test ./internal/core ./internal/route-targetcatalog -count=1 passes.
7. go test ./... -count=1 passes.

title: Instrument loop and tools with CONTRACT-001 spans
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>go-std | testing</concerns>
<practices>go fmt ./... before every commit · go test ./... and go test -race ./... for concurrent code · golangci-lint run · gosec ./... · govulncheck ./... · log with slog, wrap errors with context, and keep tests table-driven with testify</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 keeps JSONL replay separate from OTel analytics, preserves reported cost when available, uses exact runtime pricing only for known systems/route-targets, and leaves cost unknown otherwise so replay fidelity and cross-tool analytics both survive.</adrs>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 span instrumentation — wire invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool spans into loop.go and preserve JSONL replay behavior; review hx-0b94868b keeps it after the root telemetry package and upstream-service metadata work.</governing>
</context-digest>

Wire telemetry into loop.go so each run emits one invoke_agent span, each upstream-service attempt emits one chat span with stable turn/attempt indexing, and each tool execution emits one execute_tool span with standard OTel error semantics. Preserve existing JSONL replay behavior while adding trace correlation and root/session rollups.
acceptance: loop.go emits invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool spans with required turn and attempt identity, tool failures use standard OTel error semantics, JSONL replay behavior remains unchanged, and tests cover turn indexing, retry indexing, and tool span error handling

Wire telemetry into loop.go so each run emits one invoke_agent span, each upstream-service attempt emits one chat span with stable turn/attempt indexing, and each tool execution emits one execute_tool span with standard OTel error semantics. Preserve existing JSONL replay behavior while adding trace correlation and root/session rollups.
bead.acceptance: loop.go emits invoke_agent/chat/execute_tool spans with required turn and attempt identity, tool failures use standard OTel error semantics, JSONL replay behavior remains unchanged, and tests cover turn indexing, retry indexing, and tool span error handling

title: integration: retarget hx-76c04de5 closing provenance to the Bun CI fix
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:65
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit e64caefae5024770d00b609945003c10445d3f3e closes review-finding bead hx-76c04de5 with closing_commit_sha=4d247a6bb67e122181a1cc0f005e6edcd83afee4, but 4d247a6 is a later tracker-only repair for hx-f2b93e92 and touches only .ddx/beads.jsonl. The actual Bun CI remediation for hx-76c04de5 landed earlier in commit a5b8d2f0971cc4b9f99cdde438824261485f1655, which updates .github/workflows/ci.yml to use oven-sh/setup-bun, bun install, bunx playwright, and bun run test:e2e. FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as replay/audit provenance, so hx-76c04de5 now points replay at unrelated metadata instead of the implementation that satisfied its acceptance criteria.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-76c04de5.closing_commit_sha if no isolated governing fix commit exists, or retarget it to the commit that actually applies the Bun CI change and document that implementation boundary explicitly.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: preserve known root cost rollups across mixed known cost sources
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: loop.go:289
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new root-span cost rollup logic marks the invoke_agent span as unknown whenever known child chat spans disagree on ddx.cost.source, currency, or pricing_ref. That drops a valid total for mixed but fully known sessions, which violates CONTRACT-001's rule that the root cost only becomes unknown when a contributing chat span has unknown cost. Reproduced against the current code by running a two-turn session with a upstream-service-reported first turn and an exact configured-cost second turn: result.CostUSD=0.03 while the root span still emits ddx.cost.source=unknown.
Suggested fix: Roll up root ddx.cost.amount whenever every contributing chat span has known cost, and only force ddx.cost.source=unknown when a contributing span is actually unknown. If metadata such as pricing_ref or source cannot be represented cleanly at the root, omit or summarize those fields rather than discarding the known total. Add a regression test covering mixed known-cost provenance across turns.
acceptance: A session whose chat spans all have known cost preserves a root invoke_agent ddx.cost.amount equal to the sum of child costs even when provenance differs across turns; the root span only emits ddx.cost.source=unknown when at least one contributing chat span has unknown cost; automated tests cover mixed upstream-service-reported/configured cost rollups.

title: docs-corpus: add legacy routing docs grep check
description:
Add an enforceable docs-corpus guardrail for legacy DDx agent command/routing vocabulary. This is the final gate for ddx-02335e0b after active docs are scrubbed.

In-scope files:
- A new or existing docs lint/check script, Make target, or test file chosen to match repo conventions.
- A checked-in allowlist file for historical/deprecation references, if needed.
- Documentation of the check near the script/allowlist.

Required behavior:
- The check scans active docs for stale public surfaces: `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, `--profile`, `--quorum`, `--runneres`, execution-path ResolveRoute/RouteDecision, profile_ladders, and route-target_overrides.
- The check fails unallowlisted hits.
- Allowlisted hits must include a reason such as historical archive, migration note, or explicit deprecation handler.

Out of scope:
- Scrubbing the specific active docs; sibling beads own that.
- Code routinglint for Go execution paths; separate implementation beads own that.
acceptance:
1. The new docs check command passes in this repository.
2. The check fails when a temporary unallowlisted `ddx agent run` reference is added to an active docs file.
3. The allowlist, if present, names each remaining historical/deprecation hit and why it is allowed.
4. `make skill-schema` and `ddx skills check cli/internal/skills/ddx .agents/skills/ddx .claude/skills/ddx` still pass if skill files are touched.

title: FEAT-008 P7: US-086a worker live stream + tool cards + reconnect
description: Subscribe to workerProgress via graphql-ws. Live region is a NAMED <section role=region aria-label='Live response'> containing an aria-live=polite inner log — bare role=log does NOT satisfy the test. Contiguous text_delta frames MUST concatenate into readable text in the DOM (two adjacent frames render as joined text, not separate nodes). tool_call frames → collapsible <details> cards interleaved with text. On reconnect: fetch worker(id).recentEvents to catch up; show reconnect banner while bridging. Terminal state: stop appending, render completed timestamp + 'Evidence bundle' link (link must be present even if mocked evidenceBundleUrl is missing — use worker id to synthesize the path).
acceptance: US-086a.a-c green in e2e/workers.spec.ts.

title: bug: routing specs must use MinPower/MaxPower, not profiles/ladders
description:
Review/evolution finding.
File: docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md and related specs.
Category: drift
Severity: high
Description: Specs and tests must stop describing DDx-managed profiles, ladders, route-target overrides, singular requested Power, or execution-path ResolveRoute. The current requirement is power bounds: DDx owns effort estimation and bead retry policy over MinPower/MaxPower; the agent owns runner/upstream-service/route-target routing and reports actual route-target/power used.
Suggested fix: Rewrite FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-001, DDx skill docs, tests, and routing beads around MinPower/MaxPower, actual power, route-target/power catalog, and DDx-owned retry escalation.
acceptance:
1. FEAT-006, FEAT-010, FEAT-001, DDx skill docs, and routing beads describe MinPower/MaxPower instead of cheap/standard/smart profiles or singular requested Power.
2. Specs say DDx owns bead retry classification and may raise MinPower on eligible retry.
3. Specs say agent owns routing inside power bounds and reports actual route-target/power.
4. routinglint/test acceptance references the power-boundary and status/debug-only ResolveRoute.

title: routing: effort/permissions gating per upstream-service+route-target (not just per runner)
description:
## Upstream-service-specific honoring of --effort and --permissions (sibling of ddx-4817edfd).

Today, Runner.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so runneres without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But upstream-service-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning route-targets, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every runner+upstream-service combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.

## Scope

- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).
  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (upstream-service, route-target).
  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.
- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (upstream-service, route-target) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.
- Document expected behavior for each known upstream-service in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning route-targets = no effort, etc.).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).
- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go
acceptance:
1. Capability table tracks SupportsEffort and SupportsPermission per (upstream-service, route-target_pattern).
2. evaluateCandidate rejects candidates that don't honor a RouteRequest's --effort or --permissions, with a debug reason that names which capability and which upstream-service/route-target.
3. Unknown capability does NOT reject; logs at -v.
4. Unit tests cover: known-no-effort rejection, known-yes pass, unknown pass with warning, no-effort-requested disables the check (parallel structure for permissions).
5. go test ./cli/... is green.

title: correctness: preserve non-envelope JSON with kind+title
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:375
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `isPromptEnvelopeProbe` now treats any JSON object with top-level `kind` plus `title` as a DDx prompt envelope candidate. That fixes malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"..."}` envelopes, but it also re-breaks backward compatibility for plain JSON prompts whose `kind` is not the DDx envelope discriminator. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}' -upstream-service nonexistent` now returns `invalid prompt envelope: kind, id, and prompt are required` instead of passing the JSON through as raw prompt text; the same regression occurs for stdin and `@file` because all three paths call `resolvePrompt` -> `parsePromptInput`. This violates the earlier plain-JSON compatibility requirement while over-matching objects that are not DDx prompt envelopes.\nSuggested fix: narrow the probe so `title` only contributes to envelope detection when the object is actually a DDx prompt envelope candidate, for example by requiring `kind == "prompt"` before `title` triggers validation; add regression coverage proving `{"kind":"note","title":"hello"}` stays raw text for inline, stdin, and `@file`, while malformed `{"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"}` still fails validation.
acceptance: A plain JSON prompt like {"kind":"note","title":"hello"} remains raw prompt text for -p, stdin, and @file inputs, while malformed DDx prompt envelopes like {"kind":"prompt","title":"Inspect main"} still fail with an invalid prompt envelope error; regression tests cover both behaviors.

title: route-targetcatalog: bootstrap embedded route-target power values
description: Populate the embedded catalog after schema and provenance support exist. In-scope: internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog/route-targets.yaml and catalog tests only. Assign power values for the small supported starter set: local/openai-compatible Qwen route-targets known to this repo, subscription runner route-targets exposed by Claude/Codex/Gemini discovery, and selected popular OpenRouter route-targets already represented or explicitly added to the catalog. Mark older same-family route-targets exact-pin-only unless there is an explicit cost/power override reason. Apply upstream-service/deployment class rules so local/community/self-hosted route-targets do not tie managed cloud frontier route-targets solely from benchmarks.
acceptance: 1. Every embedded route-target intended for automatic routing has power in the agreed scale. 2. Route-targets with no power or older same-family status are exact_pin_only unless an override reason is present. 3. The embedded catalog includes enough local, OpenRouter, Claude, Codex, and Gemini subscription entries to support the first --list-route-targets inventory. 4. Catalog values demonstrate upstream-service/deployment class separation: local/community gpt-oss/minimax-class entries, if present, do not share frontier power with cloud Sonnet/Opus/GPT without an explicit documented override. 5. A test fails if any target candidate intended for auto-routing lacks power. 6. go test ./internal/route-targetcatalog passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: layer-2 design: should execute-bead workers create beads in-band?
description:
During investigation of ddx-7eab13a6 (bead-id resolver worktree bug), a design question surfaced: is it correct for execute-bead workers to call `ddx bead create` directly in-band?

Three alternatives:

(a) Append to parent bead. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.

(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.

(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.

Today's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.

Additionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-<parent>-<timestamp>-<random>-<hex> (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.

Scope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Also decide whether to repair the 22 existing malformed-ID beads.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: BUG: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI upstream-service error aborts agent without classification
description:
## Symptom

Sessions in `Projects/ddx/.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` aborted mid-stream with:

```
agent: upstream-service error: received error while streaming: {"message":"Error in iterating prediction stream: NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI"}
```

Observed against `surface=embedded-openai`. The agent surfaces this as a generic "upstream-service error" and aborts the session.

## Why it matters

This is a **server-capability** error — the local OpenAI-compat server (probably mlx_lm / lmstudio variant) cannot handle a quantized rotating KV cache for the route-target+config combination. The user can fix it (turn off KV quant; use a different cache type; pick a different route-target). Today the error is opaque, so the user just sees a stream abort and may retry endlessly.

## Proposed fix

In `upstream-service/openai` (or wherever the streaming SSE error is decoded):

1. Detect the `NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI` substring (or any `NotImplementedError` from the upstream-service) and classify it as a structured error, not a transport error.
2. Emit a clear human message: "Local server does not support quantized rotating KV cache for this route-target. Disable KV quantization on the server, or use a route-target/config it supports."
3. Mark the error non-retryable in the routing layer — endless retries waste compute and the failure is deterministic.
4. Optional: add a routing hint so this upstream-service+route-target combo is skipped on subsequent dispatches in the same session.

## Related

Counterpart bead being filed in lucebox-hub/dflash to add this as a known failure mode the bench should be able to provoke deliberately (it currently passes `enable_thinking: false` to avoid the related "reasoning stall" path, but doesn't surface KV-cache-incompatibility paths).
acceptance:
1. When a streaming response contains NotImplementedError: RotatingKVCache Quantization NYI (or any NotImplementedError from the upstream-service), the agent emits a structured error with a stable code (e.g. UPSTREAM-SERVICE_CAPABILITY_MISSING) and a human-readable message naming the missing capability.
2. The error is non-retryable in the routing layer — agent does not silently retry on the same upstream-service+route-target.
3. A unit test fixture replays a recorded SSE stream containing this error and asserts the structured classification.
4. Behavior verified against ddx replay of the failing session in sessions.jsonl.

title: correctness: document Playwright build prerequisites
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-002-web-stack.md:147
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated ADR now shows the embedded Playwright config launching `${cliRoot}/build/ddx server --port 18080`, but the run instructions still say only `bun run test:e2e`. That command does not build `cli/build/ddx` or refresh the embedded frontend bundle. On a clean checkout, or after frontend changes, following the ADR literally can fail because the binary is missing or stale. CI avoids this by running `bun run build` in `cli/internal/server/frontend` and `make build` in `cli` before `bun run test:e2e`.
Suggested fix: Update ADR-002's Playwright run instructions to state the required build prerequisites explicitly, or change the documented workflow to invoke a command that builds the frontend bundle and CLI binary before running Playwright.
acceptance: ADR-002 documents the required preconditions for `bun run test:e2e` so the steps mention building the frontend bundle and `cli/build/ddx` first, consistent with `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and CI.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Switch the default Playwright e2e runner to a Go-server fixture
description:
## Context

The frontend Playwright suite under `cli/internal/server/frontend` currently starts `bun run preview` on port 4173, which serves static SvelteKit output without the Go DDx backend. The parent e2e suite now includes direct API tests and route/page tests that require real DDx server data. TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts` calls `/api/health`, `/api/documents`, `/api/beads`, `/api/beads/status`, `/api/personas`, and `/api/docs/graph`; those endpoints return 404 under static preview but return 200 from the Go server.

Build a fixture-backed default Playwright runner that boots the Go DDx server on a deterministic local port and points Playwright at it. The fixture must be self-contained so e2e runs do not read the developer's home config or the repository's live `.ddx/` state.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` — change the default `webServer` to run the Go DDx server (`go run ./cli` from the repo root or a built `ddx` binary) on a deterministic port, set `use.baseURL` to that server, and keep any static-only preview coverage separate from the default backend-backed project.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/` — add a fixture project directory containing minimal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` data with open, closed, ready, and blocked beads; a tiny `docs/` library; and any config needed by the server.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — only TC-008 API-test alignment if the fixture data requires narrow expectation updates.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.demo.config.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts`.
- Go server source under `cli/internal/server/`; the API is assumed correct.
- Frontend components or routes.
- Migrating TC-001 through TC-007 page tests; those are separate child beads.

## Rollback / cleanup

Do not leave a half-populated fixture directory. Do not make tests pass by skipping or deleting them. The default test runner must use a temp copy or otherwise isolated fixture workspace, not the developer's actual repository checkout or `$HOME`.
acceptance:
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e --grep TC-008
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && ! grep -rE '\$HOME|~/' e2e/fixtures/
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && grep -E 'webServer|baseURL|ddx|go run' playwright.config.ts
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && git diff --exit-code -- playwright.demo.config.ts e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts

title: Validate full benchmark pipeline and close epic acceptance criteria
description: Run end-to-end validation of the complete ddx-agent benchmark pipeline against all five epic acceptance criteria. rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: correctness: anthropic streaming usage loses input tokens across deltas
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 51f778b.
File: upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:236, upstream-service/anthropic/anthropic.go:275, stream_consume.go:84
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Anthropic streaming emits input tokens on the initial message_start delta and output/cache tokens on later message_delta events, but consumeStream replaces resp.Usage on each delta instead of accumulating it. In the default streaming path, the final Response therefore drops the earlier input token counts and retains only the last usage fragment. Anthropic's streaming code also never sets Total, so even if the fragments were merged the run-level total token count would remain zero. This leaves llm.response, session.end, and any cost/accounting derived from streaming Anthropic responses inconsistent with the non-streaming upstream-service path.
Suggested fix: Accumulate streaming usage fragments in consumeStream instead of overwriting them, and have the Anthropic streaming path emit enough data to compute a correct final TokenUsage.Total, or emit the full merged usage on the terminal delta. Add a regression test that simulates Anthropic-style split usage deltas and asserts the final Response and emitted response event preserve input, output, cache, and total token counts.
acceptance: For Anthropic streaming runs, token usage from message_start and message_delta is merged into the final Response and llm.response event; Input, Output, CacheRead, and CacheWrite are preserved; Total equals Input+Output; a regression test covers split usage deltas

title: agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout kills thinking-route-target requests; not configurable
description:
Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30
  const DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute

This is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every upstream-service regardless of route-target class. It is set on every service path:
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           Upstream-serviceTimeout: DefaultUpstream-serviceRequestTimeout

The comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'

But that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:
  const DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute

The 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.

Concrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):

1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)
   - status: no_changes
   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)
   - log: 'WARN upstream-service error, retrying attempt=1 err="upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s" delay=1s'
   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.

2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)
   - All 3 hit 'upstream-service request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt
   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each

Why thinking route-targets hit this:
qwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning route-targets can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the runner considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the runner is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.

Why arbitrary timeouts are not helping:

a) Same value for cloud upstream-services (claude, codex) and local upstream-services (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking route-targets routinely need >15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.

b) No way to opt out of the cap per upstream-service, per route-target, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.

c) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.

d) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking route-target the timeout doesn't mean 'broken upstream-service', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.
acceptance:
AC1. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[upstream-service].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per upstream-service.

AC2. Upstream-serviceTimeout is configurable per-tier via the route-target-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking route-target (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat route-targets keep the 15-min default.

AC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultUpstream-serviceIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.

AC4. When Upstream-serviceTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').

AC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.

AC6. Default for known-thinking-route-target surfaces (upstream-service.type=lmstudio AND route-target name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Add project-scoped route middleware and migrate document/bead/graph routes
description:
## Scope
Add project-scoping middleware to the DDx server and migrate all document, bead, and doc-graph HTTP routes to canonical project-scoped paths.

## Context
The server currently registers ~50 routes under /api/... without project scoping. FEAT-002 requires /api/projects/{project}/... as canonical. Three worker routes already use this pattern (handleProjectWorkerList, handleProjectWorkerShow, handleProjectWorkerProgress) — follow that pattern.

## Implementation approach
1. Create a projectMiddleware (or helper function) that:
   - Extracts {project} from the URL path
   - Resolves it via GetProjectByID() or GetProjectByPath()
   - Injects the resolved ProjectEntry into request context
   - Returns 404 if project not found
2. Register all document, bead, and doc-graph routes under /api/projects/{project}/...
3. Keep legacy /api/... routes as singleton compatibility aliases that auto-resolve to the single registered project (error if >1 project registered without scoping)
4. Add test(s) that register two projects, verify scoped routes resolve correctly, and verify legacy routes work with one project

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/server/server.go — route registration, middleware
- cli/internal/server/handlers.go (or individual handler files) — extract project from context instead of hardcoding
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go — new project-scoped routing tests

## Files NOT to touch
- MCP tools (separate bead)
- GraphQL schema (already has beadsByProject, workersByProject)
- Frontend routes
acceptance: (1) A projectMiddleware or projectResolver extracts {project} from /api/projects/{project}/... routes and injects resolved ProjectEntry into request context. (2) Document routes (list, read, write, search), bead routes (list, show, create, update, claim, unclaim, reopen, ready, blocked, status, deps), and doc graph routes (graph, stale, deps, dependents, history, diff, show, write) are all registered under /api/projects/{project}/... in addition to existing legacy routes. (3) Legacy unscoped routes continue to work when exactly one project is registered (singleton compatibility). (4) cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run 'TestProject' -count=1 passes with at least one test covering scoped route resolution.

title: bug: route-status follow-up scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review, amended by MinPower/MaxPower requirement.
File: bead ddx-6036e1bc
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The route-status follow-up was mostly observability, but its previous scope included routing evidence events with full Candidates tied to D3 preflight. That kept ResolveRoute and candidate traces coupled to execution. Under the updated boundary, ResolveRoute is status/debug-only; execution evidence should record requested MinPower/MaxPower and actual route-target/power returned by Execute.
Suggested fix: Rescope ddx-6036e1bc to route-target/power catalog and route-status observability only. Execution evidence should record requested power bounds, effort/task facts, actual route-target/power, cost, and session metadata from Execute, not preflight candidates from ResolveRoute.
acceptance: ddx-6036e1bc or its replacement explicitly limits ResolveRoute/Candidates consumption to status/debug/UI; execute/run evidence tests assert no ResolveRoute candidate trace is produced by normal run/try/work execution; power catalog data is used only to choose MinPower thresholds, never route-target/upstream-service pins.

title: integration suite: scaffolding + initial corpus + bench runner binary
description:
Scaffold the integration / comparison suite per the plan. Lives in agent.

- cmd/ddx-agent-bench/ — runner binary. Discovers (runner, upstream-service, route-target) candidates from config, runs corpus tasks against each, captures metrics, emits report.
- bench/corpus/ — initial test corpus (versioned with repo). Initial set: single tool call (list files in cmd/), multi-tool sequence (find *.go + count lines), tool error handling (read missing file), reasoning + tool combination (largest file in dir), long-output generation (latency check).
- bench/results/ — gitignored; local result storage.

Builds on compare.go/benchmark.go/quorum.go/condense.go infrastructure (those move from DDx in a separate bead — agent-X to be filed for that move). For this bead, scaffold the bench runner against current infrastructure; rewire to upstream compare/benchmark code when those land.

Credential management: layered (CLI flags > env > project config > user config > agent defaults).

Skips runneres without credentials gracefully.
acceptance:
- [ ] cmd/ddx-agent-bench/ binary builds
- [ ] bench/corpus/ contains at least 5 task files with documented expected behavior
- [ ] Running the bench against a config with no credentials produces a clean "skipped: no credentials" report
- [ ] Running against bragi (when available) executes the corpus and reports per-task metrics

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: review: bounded evidence assembly with minimum floor + pre-dispatch short-circuit
description:
Stage C1 of FEAT-022. Modify the single review-prompt assembler
(post-collapse agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to use cli/internal/evidence
primitives for bounded assembly with a minimum evidence floor and
pre-dispatch short-circuit on residual oversize.

Implements FEAT-022 requirements §5 and §7 for the reviewer path.
Satisfies US-220 and contributes to US-225.

Depends on Stage B (ddx-c924af97) for the single assembler and Stage A
(ddx-64299657) for primitives.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (BuildReviewPrompt and
  gitShow: route governing-doc reads through evidence.ReadFileClamped,
  route diff through evidence.ClampDiff + evidence.DecomposeDiff,
  extend EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs as needed, implement priority-
  ordered assembly)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (wire pre-dispatch short-
  circuit: if assembled prompt exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all
  trimming, skip upstream-service dispatch and emit review-error:
  context_overflow directly; do NOT close the bead)
- Tests with multi-MB synthetic governing docs and multi-MB synthetic
  diffs exercising: minimum floor preservation (bead title, description,
  AC, notes, full changed-file inventory always present); governing-doc
  degradation to outline + first-N-lines when content exceeds cap; diff
  ranking (files named in AC > files matching governing ref paths >
  others); large-file degradation to stat + hunk headers; pre-dispatch
  short-circuit path.

Out-of-scope:
- Grading path (Stage C2, separate bead)
- Section-level telemetry (Stage F)
- Error-class distinction for non-overflow classes (Stage G); this bead
  introduces context_overflow as the only new class it needs
- Markdown-aware heading extraction (deferred per FEAT-022 §1; use
  line-based type-agnostic FitSections)

Preserve invariant: reviewer failure does NOT close the bead. The
pre-dispatch short-circuit emits the event and leaves the bead in the
same state as any other review-error (closed status gated elsewhere).
acceptance: cd cli && go test -run TestBuildReviewPrompt ./internal/agent/... passes including new tests for: governing-doc trimming (fixture doc = 10× MaxGoverningDocBytes, assert output bounded + truncation marker present), diff trimming (fixture diff = 10× MaxDiffBytes), minimum floor preservation under extreme pressure (fixture total input = 20× MaxPromptBytes; output still contains bead.Title, .Description, .Acceptance, .Notes, and full changed-file inventory verbatim), ranked diff inclusion (AC-referenced files appear before unreferenced files in output order), large-file stat+hunk-header degradation (single fixture file > MaxInlinedFileBytes → output contains stat line and hunk headers but not full content), pre-dispatch short-circuit. cd cli && go test -run TestReviewContextOverflow ./internal/agent/... passes: given a fixture that forces residual overflow, the executor emits a review-error event whose body contains the literal substring 'context_overflow' and the event's recorded upstream-service-dispatch count remains 0 (no upstream-service call happened). cd cli && go test -run TestReviewFailureNotCloseBead ./internal/agent/... still passes (the existing execute_bead_review_failure_modes_test.go must remain green — preservation of the non-close invariant). AST/import check (not a grep): cd cli && go test -run TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage ./internal/agent/... asserts that execute_bead_review.go imports cli/internal/evidence and that the file's AST contains no direct calls to os.ReadFile or osexec.Command("git", "show", ...) unless a parent node carries an //evidence:allow-unbounded directive comment.

title: Automate independent benchmark baseline capture for ddx-agent
description: Create automation for capturing and comparing ddx-agent benchmark results independent of vendor claims after the adapter path and fixed subset are defined. This should run the committed fixed subset of Terminal-Bench tasks (or the Harbor-compatible equivalent), emit a machine-readable report with git SHA, agent version, route-target/upstream-service, config, and per-task outcomes, and make before/after comparisons easy.
acceptance: The repo contains a reproducible benchmark command or script that runs the committed fixed benchmark subset and writes a report artifact. The report includes git SHA, ddx-agent version, route-target/upstream-service/config, per-task pass/fail or graded outcome, and aggregate resolved-task rate. Documentation explains how to establish a baseline and compare later changes against it.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Migrate bead execution and worker smoke specs to fixture data
description:
## Context

The frontend e2e specs for beads, executions, and workers require backend data and currently fail when Playwright serves static preview output without the Go DDx server. After the fixture-backed runner exists, these specs should use the seeded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, docs library, and any execution/worker fixture state available from the runner instead of relying on developer-local DDx state.

Update the backend-data smoke specs so they pass against the self-contained fixture while preserving coverage of bead lists/status, execution views, and worker-related UI/API behavior.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/executions.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/workers.spec.ts`.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/navigation.spec.ts` and `src/routes/layout.e2e.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/screenshots.spec.ts` and any visual baseline rework.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and fixture creation except reading the fixture IDs/data created by the prerequisite bead.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts` and `playwright.demo.config.ts`.
- Go server source changes under `cli/internal/server/`.
- Test deletion, `.skip`, or broad assertion weakening.

## Rollback / cleanup

If a spec needs additional fixture records, keep the fixture deterministic and self-contained. Do not point tests at the repository's live `.ddx/` tracker or developer-specific worker state.
acceptance:
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun run test:e2e e2e/beads-smoke.spec.ts e2e/executions.spec.ts e2e/workers.spec.ts
cd cli/internal/server/frontend && git diff --exit-code -- playwright.demo.config.ts e2e/demo-recording.spec.ts

title: Step 4a — ddx-agent-bench matrix subcommand
description: Add Go subcommand cmd/bench/matrix.go: ddx-agent-bench matrix --subset --profiles --runneres --reps --budget-usd --out [--resume] [--force-rerun] [--retry-budget-halted]. NO --concurrency flag (D6: hard 1 in v1). Per run (runner,profile,rep,task) tuple: (1) compute output dir; (2) on --resume skip when final_status terminal per D5; (3) acquire per-run lockfile; (4) translate profile+runner to harbor run invocation passing adapter module path explicitly; (5) honor Retry-After; (6) write report.json with the v7 D4 telemetry + final_status derived from (process_outcome, grading_outcome, reward) state machine.
acceptance: ddx-agent-bench matrix --runneres=noop,dumb_script --profiles=noop --reps=2 produces a valid matrix; SIGKILL during a run + --resume produces an identical matrix; lockfile prevents double-spend on concurrent invocations.

title: routing: filter automatic candidates by catalog power
description: Apply catalog power eligibility to automatic routing after hard pins and route-target inventory exist. In-scope: routing candidate construction/filtering and tests. Unpinned automatic routing excludes route-targets with Power=0, missing catalog entry, inactive/deprecated status unless allowed, or exact_pin_only=true. MinPower and MaxPower filter by catalog power. Exact route-target pins may use unknown or exact-pin-only route-targets if upstream-service discovery confirms availability.
acceptance: 1. Automatic routing excludes unknown-power and exact-pin-only route-targets. 2. MinPower and MaxPower filter unpinned candidate sets. 3. Exact route-target pins can still route to a discovered unknown-power route-target inside the pinned upstream-service/runner constraints. 4. Rejection traces include power_missing, below_min_power, above_max_power, and exact_pin_only reasons or equivalent typed reasons. 5. go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Power|Candidate|Filter' passes or a documented narrower equivalent passes.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Preserve native persistence for external agent runneres by default
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: external runner persistence defaults. Evidence covers current Registry BaseArgs for codex and claude and the requirement that DDx must not suppress native upstream-service stores by default.
</context-digest>
Update external runner defaults so DDx stops interfering with native session persistence.

## Scope
- Remove codex `--ephemeral` default
- Remove claude `--no-session-persistence` default
- Keep non-interactive JSON output behavior
- Add regression tests covering the new defaults
acceptance: The codex runner no longer forces --ephemeral, the claude runner no longer forces --no-session-persistence, and tests prove DDx preserves native upstream-service persistence by default

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Capture ddx-agent benchmark baseline and failure-mode characterization
description: Before setting benchmark thresholds or adding benchmark-motivated tools, run ddx-agent against a small representative task sample and document how it currently behaves. Record actual failure modes, tool-usage patterns, clarification behavior, and edit/navigation anti-patterns. Use this to calibrate downstream acceptance criteria instead of inventing threshold numbers in advance.
acceptance: A checked-in baseline artifact captures results from a fixed pilot task sample and documents: resolved-task count, clarification-question count, shell anti-pattern usage, structured-edit usage, and notable runner/runtime failures. Downstream benchmark beads use this baseline to justify their thresholds and task-set choices.

description: Review finding hx-ca0bcad8: EventCompactionStart EventType is defined in agent.go:125 but never emitted in loop.go. rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: preserve doctor audit for legacy untyped plugin installs
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/doctor.go:454
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` now skips any installed entry whose `type` is empty unless the package name exists in `BuiltinRegistry()`. That fixes resource false-positives, but it also suppresses `ddx doctor --plugins` for legacy or third-party plugin installs whose older `installed.yaml` rows lack `type`. The project design explicitly keeps a builtin-registry fallback for legacy packages and expects old packages to remain auditable during the migration period, so this filter drops coverage for a supported compatibility path.
Suggested fix: When `entry.Type` is empty, infer plugin-like roots from the recorded files or installed root path instead of skipping outright, and add a regression test for an untyped installed plugin entry that still produces manifest/structure findings.
acceptance: Given an installed plugin entry with an empty type field and plugin-root files, when ddx doctor --plugins runs, then it still audits that entry and reports manifest or structure problems instead of silently skipping it

title: runner-test: decide PTY cassette transport for live TUI record/replay
description:
Make the transport decision for golden-master testing of interactive runneres before building cassette-heavy integration tests. The prior discussion rejected a small incidental tmux dependency: either the project standardizes on tmux for the whole runner lifecycle, or it owns PTY lifecycle directly. If the reusable cassette player is generic enough to be its own project, define that boundary instead of burying it inside agent.

The decision must evaluate:
- direct PTY ownership using Go primitives and os/exec
- tmux as the session/process lifecycle manager
- existing tools such as ntm or other terminal/TUI snapshot approaches if locally relevant
- how to record input/output bytes, screen snapshots, environment, resize events, exit status, timing, and structured service events
- how replay is deterministic without credentials
- how record mode fails fast when the target runner lacks credentials/auth/subscription/quota
- how users can inspect an in-flight or recorded run without corrupting the cassette

This bead does not implement the cassette runner. It creates the architecture decision and follow-up constraints for implementation beads.
acceptance:
1. A design or ADR document under docs/helix/02-design records the chosen PTY/cassette transport and explicitly rejects the alternatives with reasons.
2. The decision states whether tmux is a required dependency for runner execution, not a partial helper, or whether agent owns PTY lifecycle directly.
3. The decision defines the cassette data contract: input events, output bytes or frames, terminal size, environment scrub rules, exit status, timing normalization, service events, final metadata, usage, quota probe output, and versioning.
4. The decision defines record-mode credential failure behavior and replay-mode deterministic behavior.
5. CONTRACT-003 or linked design docs reference the decision from the runner integration-testing section.
6. go test ./... passes if code or generated docs are touched.

title: upstream-service: move discovery limits and cost ownership
description: Move upstream-service-specific route-target discovery, limit discovery, capabilities, and cost attribution out of the shared OpenAI-compatible implementation per docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-19-upstream-service-routing-tool-output.md.
acceptance:
1. OpenRouter route-target limit discovery and cost attribution live under upstream-service/openrouter, not the shared SDK.
2. LM Studio /api/v0/route-targets and /api/v0/route-targets/{route-target} behavior lives under upstream-service/lmstudio.
3. oMLX /v1/route-targets/status behavior and stream quirks live under upstream-service/omlx.
4. Capability claims are supplied by upstream-service wrappers rather than a shared runtime flavor table.
5. Session usage, replay, telemetry pricing, and tests use upstream-service type/openrouter/openai/etc. instead of openai-compat.
6. Conformance tests cover all concrete wrappers with fake servers.

description: Produce docs/research/route-target-census-2026-04-XX.md with one row per candidate (route-target, upstream-service, release/refresh date, output $/Mtok, input $/Mtok, cached-input $/Mtok if applicable, max output tokens, tool-use quality, runner compatibility for codex/claude-code/opencode/pi/ddx-agent, route-target-snapshot-id). Apply the v7 selection-fallback hierarchy in order: (1) tool-use viable; (2) in bracket (output <= $3/Mtok); (3) recency (>=2025-11-01). Exclusions documented for audit trail. Pick Phase A.1 anchor + Phase A.2 second route-target from surviving rows; vendors must differ across A.1 anchor and A.2 second. Excluded by recency: GPT-4o, GPT-4.1-mini, original Sonnet/Haiku/Gemini-2.5-Flash. Excluded by bracket: Sonnet 4.6, Opus, GPT-5 full.
acceptance: Doc lands; selection-fallback hierarchy applied per row with outcomes; user signs off on (anchor, second-route-target) pair. If no kept candidate exists for a vendor, that vendor is dropped from Phase A — not substituted with non-compliant fallback.

title: Delete cmd/agent routeUpstream-service and add structural boundary test
description:
Step 3 of agent-9d120ece's staged plan. After the service owns route-target_routes failover (agent-6dd4ad97) and the CLI drops PreResolved (agent-873081a9), cmd/agent/routing_upstream-service.go's routeUpstream-service type and its execution-path methods are dead code. Delete them.

Specifically remove from cmd/agent:
- routeUpstream-service type and its Chat / ChatStream / RoutingReport methods
- newRouteUpstream-service, withRoute-targetOverride, buildCandidate, recordAttempt, markCandidateFailure, routeError, shouldFailover
- cmd/agent/route_upstream-service_test.go in full
- routeUpstream-service-touching parts of cmd/agent/routing_internal_test.go

Keep helpers used by routing_smart.go for the route-status display (the user-facing 'ddx agent route' output).

Add a structural boundary test in cmd/agent/service_boundary_test.go that fails if cmd/agent/routing_upstream-service.go re-introduces an agentcore.Upstream-service-implementing type, so the runner cannot false-positive close a future route-resolution bead by passing a regression gate while the structural requirement is unmet.

Depends on: agent-873081a9 (CLI must not call into routeUpstream-service before its deletion).

In-scope files:
- cmd/agent/routing_upstream-service.go (delete or trim to route-status helpers only)
- cmd/agent/route_upstream-service_test.go (delete)
- cmd/agent/routing_internal_test.go (trim routeUpstream-service sections)
- cmd/agent/service_boundary_test.go (add structural check)

Out of scope:
- Behavior changes; this is a deletion + boundary test bead.
acceptance:
1. cmd/agent does not contain a type implementing agentcore.Upstream-service (Chat/ChatStream); the only upstream-service implementations live behind the service boundary.
2. New TestCLIRoutingUpstream-serviceHasNoCoreUpstream-serviceImpl boundary test fails when routeUpstream-service-style code is re-introduced and passes today.
3. Tests pass: go test ./cmd/agent ./... -run "Test(CLI.*Route|RouteStatus|ResolveRoute|ServiceContractUsesTypedEventDecoder|MainDoesNotImportOrCallInternalCoreRun|InternalImportBoundaryAllowlist|RoutingUpstream-serviceHasNoCoreUpstream-serviceImpl)"

title: Visibility 6: ddx agent route-status command
description:
Add a new DDx CLI subcommand `ddx agent route-status`. Mirrors ddx-agent's `cmdRouteStatus`. Shows: current routing state, recent routing decisions (which upstream-service was selected for which route-target-ref and why), health cooldowns currently in effect. Uses whatever public API ddx-agent exposes for its own route-status command — if the API is internal, file an upstream bead on ddx-agent to expose it before implementing the DDx wrapper.

Implementation lives in cli/cmd/agent_route_status.go. Uses the Go package, not shell-out.
acceptance: (1) `ddx agent route-status` output matches `ddx-agent route-status` output. (2) Shows current route table, recent decisions, active health cooldowns. (3) Uses Go package imports only. (4) `go build ./...` passes. (5) Manual: compare side-by-side with `ddx-agent route-status`.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Add Gemini PTY quota probe and routing guard
description:
Implement real Gemini interactive quota tracking before Gemini can be promoted to automatic routing.

Context:
- A real PTY session against installed Gemini CLI 0.38.2 showed /stats route-target only reports route-target/session usage after an API call. It did not show quota remaining/limit/reset for the active concrete route-target.
- The same PTY session showed /route-target manage is the quota surface. Observed output included: Flash 4% used, reset 9:13 PM (23h 46m); Flash Lite 0% used, reset 9:27 PM (24h); Pro 100% used.
- Upstream Gemini CLI docs say /stats route-target shows token counts and quota information, but the installed CLI renders quota in the route-target management dialog. Treat both /stats route-target and /route-target manage as version-sensitive PTY surfaces.
- Auth/account freshness is not quota. Do not promote Gemini to AutoRoutingEligible=true until parsed quota evidence includes remaining/limit or used percent plus reset time by route-target/tier, with freshness and source recorded.

In-scope files:
- internal/runneres/gemini/ or internal/runneres/ptyquota/ for the PTY driver/parser/cassette fixture.
- service.go and service_capabilities.go for surfacing Gemini quota state and routing eligibility.
- service_status_test.go, service_route_attempts_test.go, service_test.go, and relevant Gemini/PTY tests.
- docs/helix/02-design/primary-runner-capability-baseline.md and docs/helix/02-design/runner-golden-integration.md for evidence updates.

Out-of-scope:
- Do not treat GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, OAuth files, or account tier as quota.
- Do not promote Gemini to primary/auto-routing unless routing consumes the parsed quota cache and blocks exhausted tiers.
- Do not change Codex or Claude quota behavior except shared helper refactors required by the Gemini parser.
acceptance:
1. Add a Gemini PTY quota probe or cassette-backed parser that can parse /route-target manage output containing tier/route-target usage percentages and reset times, including exhausted tiers such as Pro 100% used.
2. Persist parsed Gemini quota evidence in a durable cache with source, captured_at, freshness, per-tier/route-target used percent or remaining/limit, and reset time.
3. ListRunneres reports Gemini quota only from parsed quota evidence, never from auth/account freshness alone. Missing or stale Gemini quota evidence is not ok.
4. Automatic routing does not select Gemini when quota evidence is missing/stale or the selected tier/route-target is exhausted; if promotion is enabled, it is gated by fresh parsed quota evidence.
5. Add tests covering the observed PTY output: Flash 4% used with reset, Flash Lite 0% used with reset, and Pro 100% used/exhausted.
6. go test ./... passes.

title: land safety: surface preserved-needs-review state and operator docs
description:
Parent: ddx-efe058be. Wire the safety gates into operator-facing state and docs. When large-deletion, syntax, or post-land gates preserve a result, the bead should clearly surface preserved-needs-review with the preserve ref, file/path summary, and next operator action. Add documentation explaining the gates and explicit override language for intentional large deletions.

In-scope files:
- CLI/server code that records bead notes/events for preserved safety-gate results
- docs/ operational documentation
- tests for notes/event content if the tracker API supports it

Out-of-scope:
- Adding new safety gates
- Rewriting existing execute-loop taxonomy beyond what is needed to expose preserved-needs-review.
acceptance:
1. A safety-gate preserved result records an operator-visible note or event containing preserved-needs-review, preserve_ref, and a one-line gate summary.
2. docs/ documents the large-deletion override language and what preserved-needs-review means operationally.
3. Tests cover the emitted event/note shape where practical.
4. Relevant Go tests and docs lint, if present, pass.
