title: refactor(svelte): migrate to houdini@next or commit to STACK.md Option A/B before Stage 4.12
description:
Opus Stage 3 batch review (2026-04-15): Houdini 1.5.10 has unresolved peer-dep incompatibility with vite 8 + kit 2.57. STACK.md recommended Option A (downgrade vite) or Option B (drop Houdini for graphql-request). Stage 3.4 shipped with neither — works for now because the single NodeInfo query is trivial.

Stage 4.12 Workers page requires graphql-ws subscriptions (workerProgress). Houdini 1.5.10 subscription support on Vite 8 is the most likely place the peer-dep workaround falls apart.

Decision required BEFORE Stage 4.12: either (a) migrate to houdini@next (removes the 'upgrade to houdini@next' noise and fixes Vite 8 support), or (b) drop Houdini entirely for graphql-request + graphql-ws (lower abstraction, no codegen).
acceptance: (1) Decision recorded in STACK.md. (2) Either houdini@next installed and regen passes, or graphql-request + graphql-ws wired up with equivalent code paths. (3) bun run build succeeds without peer-dep warnings. (4) Stage 4.12 workerProgress subscription round-trips in a smoke test.

title: Remove lossy agent_runner mirror from .ddx/config.yaml and close env-var gap in native config path
description:
Remove DDx's lossy embedded-agent config mirror and close the env-var forwarding gap in the native config path.

## Context

FEAT-006 defines native `.agent/config.yaml` as the authoritative config source for the embedded runner. The current `.ddx/config.yaml` `agent_runner` section is a deprecated mirror that duplicates native runtime config fields. Its precedence semantics are documented in FEAT-006 "Embedded Runner Configuration Ownership".

## Goals

- Remove `AgentRunnerConfig` type and `agent_runner` YAML fields from `.ddx/config.yaml` schema once no project uses it
- Remove `AgentYAMLConfig`, `LLMPresetYAML` mirror types from `cli/internal/agent/runner.go`
- Remove the `AgentConfigLoader` injection hook and `resolveAgentConfig` fallback path from `agent_runner.go`
- Apply `AGENT_*` env vars (`AGENT_ROUTE-TARGET`, `AGENT_UPSTREAM-SERVICE`, `AGENT_BASE_URL`, `AGENT_API_KEY`, `AGENT_PRESET`) when resolving from native config, closing the gap documented in `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath`
- Wire the `agentRunner()` cmd factory to pass a working-directory path to native config resolution instead of loading the `agent_runner` block
- Update `TestConfigPrecedenceEnvVarAppliesInDdxFallbackPath` to verify env vars work in the native path after the fix

## Acceptance Criteria

- `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` pass with no regressions
- `.ddx/config.yaml` no longer accepts or uses `agent_runner` fields
- `AGENT_ROUTE-TARGET` set in the environment overrides the route-target resolved from native `.agent/config.yaml`
- Existing `.ddx/config.yaml` files with `agent_runner` blocks emit a deprecation warning or are silently ignored (no crash)
- The migration path in FEAT-006 remains valid
acceptance: agent_runner mirror removed from DDx config schema; AGENT_* env vars apply in native config path; all tests pass

title: Phase 5: migrate agent_cmd.go + server/workers.go routing to service.ResolveRoute
description:
Discovered while preparing the file-delete beads (ddx-f0cab7b3 etc.) — there are unplanned legacy-routing callers blocking the deletion of routing.go from DDx:

cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go uses agent.NormalizeRouteRequest + agent.RankCandidates + agent.SelectBestCandidate + runner.ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans at lines 304, 321-323, 1508-1511, 1543-1558.

cli/internal/server/workers.go uses the same primitives at lines 461-475.

Both should migrate to service.ResolveRoute (per CONTRACT-003 — single routing engine consolidated in agent-1a486c2e). After this lands the file-delete beads (ddx-ea7f041c routing.go, ddx-f0cab7b3 state+registry, ddx-70c48576 routing observability, ddx-1bf6c0df tier_escalation, ddx-2912e7cd discovery+deadline+probe) become unblocked.
acceptance:
- [ ] cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go has zero references to NormalizeRouteRequest/RankCandidates/SelectBestCandidate/BuildCandidatePlans/ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans
- [ ] cli/internal/server/workers.go same
- [ ] Both call service.ResolveRoute (or service.Execute with bare profile/route-target-ref) and consume RouteDecision
- [ ] All affected tests pass
- [ ] DDx builds clean

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: correctness: move semantic failures out of route feedback
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1313 and docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1371
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: CONTRACT-003 says DDx reports semantic task outcomes through RecordRouteAttempt and that route-target-quality/task-failure contributes to route-target/runner reliability for future ranking. The clarified boundary is that agent does not track semantic success/failure directly. Agent only reports availability/network/protocol-level failure. Semantic task outcomes belong to the caller and may be contributed to the route-target catalog or catalog-derived power rating, not to agent's transient route feedback store.
Suggested fix: Remove route-target-quality/task-failure from RouteAttempt route-health semantics, redefine RecordRouteAttempt or a separate catalog-feedback mechanism for caller-provided semantic evidence, and document how that evidence affects catalog power ratings outside the agent dispatch loop.
acceptance: CONTRACT-003 no longer states that semantic task failures directly demote route candidates in agent; docs distinguish upstream-service availability feedback from caller-owned semantic catalog feedback; failure classes exclude route-target-quality/task-failure from agent route-health penalties

bead.title: correctness: move semantic failures out of route feedback
bead.description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1313 and docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service.md:1371
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: CONTRACT-003 says DDx reports semantic task outcomes through RecordRouteAttempt and that route-target-quality/task-failure contributes to route-target/runner reliability for future ranking. The clarified boundary is that agent does not track semantic success/failure directly. Agent only reports availability/network/protocol-level failure. Semantic task outcomes belong to the caller and may be contributed to the route-target catalog or catalog-derived power rating, not to agent's transient route feedback store.
Suggested fix: Remove route-target-quality/task-failure from RouteAttempt route-health semantics, redefine RecordRouteAttempt or a separate catalog-feedback mechanism for caller-provided semantic evidence, and document how that evidence affects catalog power ratings outside the agent dispatch loop.
bead.acceptance: CONTRACT-003 no longer states that semantic task failures directly demote route candidates in agent; docs distinguish upstream-service availability feedback from caller-owned semantic catalog feedback; failure classes exclude route-target-quality/task-failure from agent route-health penalties

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: land safety: post-land build gate reverts failed automatic lands
description:
Parent: ddx-efe058be. Add the final safety net: after an automatic land advances the target ref, run the project-level post-land verification command from .ddx/config.yaml or a conservative default, and automatically revert the local target ref if the command fails. Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations for operator review.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go and tests, or server worker landing wrapper if that is the correct ownership point
- config schema/types if a post-land check command must be introduced

Out-of-scope:
- Implementing every project-specific build command
- Changing execute-bead post-run checks before land
- Remote force-push behavior; never force-push.
acceptance:
1. A regression test configures a failing post-land command and verifies the target branch is restored to its pre-land SHA.
2. The result is preserved under refs/ddx/iterations with a reason naming the failed post-land gate.
3. A passing command leaves the target branch advanced.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestLand_.*PostLand' passes.

title: routing-overrides-e2e: --runner/--route-target/--profile flag plumbing + override e2e tests
description: Cover D5 + AC #5 from ddx-fdd3ea36. CLI surface plumbing: ddx agent run --runner X / --route-target Y / --profile Z must route through unchanged to ResolveRoute(Profile, Runner, Route-target) and surface upstream typed errors verbatim. ddx work shares the same plumbing. Touched files (expected): cli/cmd/agent_run.go, cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go, plus any flag-spec helpers.
acceptance: 1. ddx agent run --runner claude → ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Runner: "claude"). E2E test mocks gate failures (binary missing, quota exhausted) and asserts typed error surfaces; no silent substitution. 2. ddx agent run --route-target opus-4.7 → ResolveRoute(Profile: "default", Route-target: "opus-4.7"). E2E test seeds 3 upstream-services at different cost with shared route-target; asserts lowest-cost pick. 3. ddx agent run --runner claude --route-target opus-4.7 → typed ErrRunnerRoute-targetIncompatible when invalid; clean dispatch otherwise. E2E coverage of both branches. 4. ddx agent run --profile local --runner claude → upstream typed error ("claude is non-local, profile=local is local-only"); DDx surfaces it unmodified. E2E test. 5. ddx work shares the same plumbing — at least one e2e test exercises it.

title: bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop
description:
The embedded 'agent' runner can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.

## Observed

Running 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --runner agent --route-target qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):

1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.

2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:

    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    ...

3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.

4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same runner/route-target and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

## Expected

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

## Scope

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget

## Evidence

- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master
- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally
- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic
- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable
description:
Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. This is wrong on multiple axes:

1. Push race is fundamentally recoverable: pull/merge/push is the standard fix and takes seconds.
2. A 1-year cooldown means the bead never re-runs in any reasonable working horizon.
3. The work is already done locally — the bead is functionally complete; reopening it for a year is misleading.

Observed in this session on ddx-98e6e9ef (routing-preflight-gate): worker landed commit 978131c6, push rejected because origin/main had advanced (687fb4ca + 4a6c6e8a pushed remotely between the local land and the push). Loop set retry_after=2027-04-29T03:43:59Z. Operator had to manually merge, push, and clear the cooldown.

What is needed:

1. push_failed should auto-pull/merge/retry-push at least once before giving up. The merge case where origin advanced with non-overlapping work is the common case in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup.

2. If the auto-merge has conflicts that the loop cannot resolve, push_failed should set a SHORT cooldown (5-15 min), not 1 year. The operator just needs time to resolve the conflict.

3. push_failed-with-conflicts should mark the bead as needing-human-review (a structured outcome similar to declined_needs_decomposition from ddx-fba752b9), not as a generic execution failure.

4. The loop's cooldown duration should NEVER exceed some reasonable cap (e.g. 24h) for any outcome. Year-scale cooldowns mean 'never' and that should be a deliberate operator choice via 'ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...', not an automatic loop decision.
acceptance: 1. push_failed flow attempts auto pull --rebase / merge / retry-push at least once before exiting with push_failed. 2. push_failed cooldown caps at 24h regardless of underlying error. 3. push_failed-with-conflicts (auto-merge fails) emits a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed; bead is parked for human review with the conflict context recorded as a structured event (kind:push-conflict). 4. Regression test: a fake git that returns non-fast-forward on first push, then fast-forward after pull/merge, exercises the new flow and asserts the bead lands cleanly without operator intervention. 5. Regression test: a fake git that produces an unresolvable merge conflict exercises the structured-outcome path and asserts cooldown <= 24h. 6. CHANGELOG entry. 7. Update ddx-fba752b9's CHANGELOG section (or a new section) noting that the 24h cap applies to all loop-set cooldowns, not just push failures.

title: Migrate TC-001 dashboard tests to fixture node/project routes
description:
## Context

TC-001 in `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` still assumes that visiting `/` renders a Dashboard `h1`. That is stale. Since Stage 3.8 commit `3bf655cf`, `src/routes/+page.svelte` redirects to `/nodes/<node-id>` when a node is selected, and the app's concrete project views live under `/nodes/<fixture-node-id>/projects/<fixture-project-id>/...`. `e2e/navigation.spec.ts` already contains the route shape to mirror around line 177.

After the fixture-backed Playwright runner exists, rewrite only the TC-001 Dashboard tests so they navigate through the fixture node/project route and assert the current UI behavior. Keep the test intent: dashboard-level smoke coverage should prove the shell and expected project entry points render, but it must not depend on `/` being a Dashboard page.

## In-scope files

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/app.spec.ts` — TC-001 block only, currently around lines 9-57.

## Out-of-scope

- `cli/internal/server/frontend/playwright.config.ts` and `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/fixtures/`; those belong to the runner prerequisite.
- TC-002, TC-003, TC-005, TC-007, and TC-008 in `e2e/app.spec.ts`.
- `cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/navigation.spec.ts` except as a read-only reference for route patterns.

title: correctness: preserve unrelated closing provenance when finalizing review beads
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:178
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit e61d84c36aa74448ecadb2ad9084767cd453bfe0 correctly finalizes review bead hx-9c1a1558, but the same tracker diff also rewrites unrelated closed bead hx-ddx-agent-target-verify and drops its existing closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e. FEAT-004 requires unknown fields to be preserved on read/write operations, and FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as durable replay/audit provenance. Losing that field on an unrelated closed bead silently corrupts the replay baseline for the SD-018 verification task even though this commit did not govern that bead. Suggested fix: repair hx-ddx-agent-target-verify so its closing_commit_sha is restored to 2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and add/adjust tracker update coverage so finalizing one bead cannot clobber preserved fields on unrelated rows.
acceptance: ddx bead show hx-ddx-agent-target-verify --json reports closing_commit_sha=2b69dbbf9127e98d694a4bd7d07e916458c50e8e, and regression coverage proves updating or closing hx-9c1a1558 leaves unrelated bead fields unchanged

title: integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:518
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.
acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always in a git repo and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' clean, and 'git show HEAD -- .ddx/beads.jsonl' includes the bead row with closing_commit_sha equal to 'git rev-parse HEAD' rather than requiring an extra uncommitted worktree update

title: route-targetcatalog: add power schema and eligibility helpers
description: Implement the catalog schema foundation for power routing. In-scope: internal/route-targetcatalog/manifest.go, internal/route-targetcatalog/catalog.go, internal/route-targetcatalog/*_test.go. Add Route-targetEntry power metadata and exact-pin-only eligibility semantics as specified by CONTRACT-003. Route-targets with power 0 or missing power are inspectable and exact-pin-capable but not eligible for automatic routing. Do not populate the embedded catalog in this bead except minimal test fixtures.

title: correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.
acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.

bead.title: correctness: preserve closing provenance for legitimate .ddx-only work
bead.description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 71f97be suppresses closing_commit_sha whenever the landed close commit touches only paths under .ddx/. That is too broad: .ddx contains real governed artifacts such as config and other tracked workspace files, not just tracker backfills. In a temp repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, creating a bead, modifying a tracked .ddx/notes.txt file, and running ddx bead close lands a close commit that changes .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/notes.txt, but the bead still omits closing_commit_sha. FEAT-019 only says to omit metadata-only tracker backfills; dropping provenance for legitimate .ddx-scoped work makes replay/audit fall back to sessionless mode even though the implementation boundary is available.
Suggested fix: Detect metadata-only close commits more narrowly, for example by distinguishing pure .ddx/beads.jsonl backfills from other tracked .ddx artifacts, and add regression coverage for a close commit that lands tracked .ddx work.
bead.acceptance: With git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, closing a bead whose landed implementation changes only tracked .ddx/* files still records closing_commit_sha for the landed close commit, and ddx agent replay can use that commit boundary instead of falling back to sessionless/manual mode.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/cmd/bead.go:649
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.
acceptance: A review bead that already carries a valid closing_commit_sha keeps that SHA after ddx bead close when no new close commit lands, while stale prior-HEAD tracker provenance is still removed for metadata-only review closes

title: service: implement DdxAgent.ListUpstream-services + HealthCheck with polymorphic Target
description:
Implement two methods together since HealthCheck for Target.Type=upstream-service exercises the same probe code as ListUpstream-services status:

ListUpstream-services(ctx) → []Upstream-serviceInfo where each entry includes Name, Type, BaseURL, Status (connected/unreachable/error), Route-targetCount, Capabilities, IsDefault, DefaultRoute-target, CooldownState (nil if not in cooldown).

HealthCheck(ctx, target) where target = {Type: "runner"|"upstream-service", Name: string}. Triggers a fresh probe and updates internal state (so subsequent ListX reflects it). Returns nil if reachable, error otherwise.

CooldownState surfaces the same data the agent uses internally for routing demotion. Read from the routing state store (ports from DDx routing_signals.go in subsequent beads).
acceptance:
- [ ] ListUpstream-services returns one Upstream-serviceInfo per configured upstream-service with accurate live status
- [ ] IsDefault matches default_upstream-service config
- [ ] DefaultRoute-target matches per-upstream-service configured default
- [ ] CooldownState is populated when applicable (test induces cooldown)
- [ ] HealthCheck(upstream-service) updates internal state observable via subsequent ListUpstream-services
- [ ] HealthCheck(runner) routes to the runner implementation

title: correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.
Suggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance route-target that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.
acceptance: ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Decision: tmux quota refresh ownership — agent owns it; remove DDx routing_signal_tmux.go duplicate
description:
Per codex review: DDx routing_signal_tmux.go (with ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux, ReadCodexQuotaViaTmux, ANSI strip, output parsers) is duplicated by agent internal/runneres/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go (commit 8099293). DDx should not keep parallel tmux scrape parsers long-term.

Two paths:
A. Agent owns tmux scrape; DDx triggers refresh via service.HealthCheck(runner target). The agent does the tmux invocation internally; DDx never shells out tmux. Delete DDx routing_signal_tmux.go.
B. Remove the DDx-side doctor refresh path entirely. Quota state populates lazily via the agents normal probe cadence; doctor --routing reads from cache only.

Recommendation: A. service.HealthCheck(runner) already triggers a fresh probe internally (per the implementation report from agent-3afb76bb). The tmux refresh becomes implicit when DDx asks for HealthCheck on claude/codex. No new contract surface needed.

After this decision: routing_signal_tmux.go can delete from DDx (folded into the file-delete cascade); doctor --routing migration uses service.HealthCheck instead of explicit RefreshXQuotaViaTmuxForWorkDir.
acceptance:
- [ ] Decision recorded with rationale
- [ ] Verified that service.HealthCheck(runner) triggers fresh tmux probe internally (or files an agent-side bead if it doesnt)
- [ ] doctor --routing migration bead updated with the chosen approach

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: bug: compaction loop can enter stuck state — no_compaction=true repeating without bound
description:
The compaction package can enter a stuck state where compaction.end events fire with no_compaction=true and success=false repeatedly, pulsing the inactivity timer on every iteration so the caller's timeout never fires. Downstream consumer DDx ran into this mid-session on a production worker: ~8461 stuck-compaction events accumulated before the hosting process had to be killed.

## Evidence

DDx-side session log:
/Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
Parent process killed at PID 4141811 after wedging for hours on a completed task.

## DDx-side workaround (defense in depth, does not fix the root cause)

Commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 in ddx repo (bead ddx-25a41f51) wraps the embedded agent call with two guards:
1. compaction.start and compaction.end(no_compaction=true) events no longer pulse the caller's inactivity timer — only real-progress events (LLM calls, tool results, commits) extend the timeout.
2. Circuit breaker counts consecutive compaction.end(success=false, no_compaction=true) events and aborts with a structured compaction_stuck error after a configurable threshold (default 50).

The workaround catches the stuck state at the DDx/caller layer. The native fix belongs in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent so every ddx-agent user (not just DDx CLI) benefits.

## Requested upstream fix

- Compaction driver detects its own stuck state via the same heuristic (consecutive success=false/no_compaction=true events) and returns a structured error to the caller
- Eliminates the need for the caller to interpret compaction events as activity signals
- Exposes a clean API signal so consumers can decide whether to retry, escalate, or abort
- Regression test seeds the stuck state and verifies the driver bails instead of looping

## Scope

- Bounded attempt counter inside the compaction loop (not just the caller)
- Structured error: compaction.ErrStuck or similar
- Integration test using a deterministic seed that reproduces the 33MB jsonl pattern

## Relationship to DDx

When this lands upstream, DDx can remove its circuit-breaker workaround and rely on the native error. Until then, DDx keeps both layers as belt-and-suspenders.
acceptance: (1) Compaction loop bounded with a structural stop. (2) Native structured error signals stuck state to callers. (3) Regression test reproduces the stuck state via seeded read-only tool calls. (4) DDx repo can remove the caller-side circuit breaker once this upstream fix is consumed.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: beadbench: pre-warm vidar MLX before paired runs
description:
First-run cold route-target load on vidar burns ~7 minutes (turns 1-2 at 15-16 tps, ~200s each, while ~27GB Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit weights load into VRAM). On a 15-min sub-run budget that's ~47% gone before any useful work.

Mitigation: send a tiny prompt (e.g. 'pong' completion at max_tokens=1) to each upstream-service+route-target pair in the manifest during preflight, before the timed sub-runs start. The route-target then stays resident across sub-runs in the same beadbench invocation.

Forensic source: run-20260429T003337Z-97454/agent-nav-tools__agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36__r1 — turn-1 latency 200.6s for 3209 tokens (16 tps), turn-2 latency 226.8s (15 tps), turn-3 latency 11.5s (302 tps effective). The 12.5x speedup at turn 3 confirms the bottleneck is one-time load, not per-request caching.

bead.title: beadbench: pre-warm vidar MLX before paired runs
bead.description:
First-run cold route-target load on vidar burns ~7 minutes (turns 1-2 at 15-16 tps, ~200s each, while ~27GB Qwen3.6-27B-MLX-8bit weights load into VRAM). On a 15-min sub-run budget that's ~47% gone before any useful work.

Mitigation: send a tiny prompt (e.g. 'pong' completion at max_tokens=1) to each upstream-service+route-target pair in the manifest during preflight, before the timed sub-runs start. The route-target then stays resident across sub-runs in the same beadbench invocation.

Forensic source: run-20260429T003337Z-97454/agent-nav-tools__agent-omlx-vidar-qwen36__r1 — turn-1 latency 200.6s for 3209 tokens (16 tps), turn-2 latency 226.8s (15 tps), turn-3 latency 11.5s (302 tps effective). The 12.5x speedup at turn 3 confirms the bottleneck is one-time load, not per-request caching.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: prefix-aware compaction still overflows when summary exceeds remaining budget
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/summarize.go:114; compaction/summarize.go:326; compaction/summarize.go:363; compaction/summarize.go:377
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The last-commit fix retries compaction with later cut points until only the active user prompt is preserved, but it never budgets the generated summary itself. Summarize still allows at least 512 output tokens regardless of remaining window, and compactMessages returns the best compacted history even when result.TokensAfter plus prefix tokens is still above the effective limit. A direct repro in the current tree with ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, a 120-character system prompt prefix (prefixTokens=32), five 120-character history turns, and a mock summarizer that returns 100 characters produces TokensAfter=89 with the final compacted request reduced to only DO-THE-THING plus the injected summary. That still exceeds the 80-token budget the commit claims to enforce.
Suggested fix: budget the summary against the remaining window after prefix tokens, preserved messages, and wrapper overhead are accounted for; trim or bound the injected summary when needed, and do not return a successful compaction result whose final request still exceeds the effective window. Add a regression test that uses the 120-character prefix / 100-character summary repro and asserts the final compacted request stays within budget while preserving the active prompt.
acceptance: With ContextWindow=80, ReserveTokens=0, EffectivePercent=100, KeepRecentTokens=20, a 120-character system-prefix token estimate, and a mock summarizer returning 100 characters, NewCompactor preserves the active user prompt and returns a compacted request whose TokensAfter is <= 80; add regression coverage for this exact overflow case.

title: routing: score candidates by power cost speed and quota
description: Implement the utility ranking for eligible automatic candidates after power filtering is correct. In-scope: routing score code and tests. Score should prefer sufficient/high power, prepaid/free/local effective cost when viable, lower marginal cost, healthy quota/reset state, availability, and speed/latency. Upstream-service/deployment class is already folded into catalog power; scoring must not treat a local/community route-target as equivalent to cloud frontier simply because one benchmark is high. It must not widen hard pins or retry. Keep weights deterministic and expose score components for trace output.
acceptance: 1. Tests prove higher-power prepaid frontier wins when effective marginal cost is low and quota is healthy. 2. Tests prove local/free candidates are selected over paid cloud when they satisfy requested power, tools, context, and availability constraints. 3. Tests prove local/free preference does not override MinPower or exact route-target/upstream-service-source/endpoint/runner pins. 4. Tests prove no-power default routing chooses the best/cheapest viable auto-routable route-target from discovered inventory. 5. Tests prove cheaper/faster candidates break ties inside the requested power range. 6. Tests prove upstream-service/deployment class power separation prevents benchmark-only local/community route-targets from tying cloud frontier route-targets. 7. Score components are included in RouteDecision candidate trace. 8. go test ./internal/routing ./... -run 'Score|Power|Cost|Quota|Latency|Local' passes or a documented narrower equivalent passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: compaction can summarize away the active user prompt when prefix exhausts keep budget
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: compaction/compactor.go:48; compaction/config.go:74; loop.go:89
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The fix subtracts non-persisted system-prompt tokens directly from KeepRecentTokens and clamps the result to zero. When the system prompt is larger than the keep budget, FindCutPoint now returns len(messages), so CompactMessages summarizes the entire live history, including the current user prompt. A repro with ContextWindow=25, KeepRecentTokens=5, and an 80-character system prompt shows the final upstream-service call receives only the system prompt plus the generated summary; the explicit user prompt  is no longer present as a real user message. SD-006 says session prefix messages are excluded from preserved user messages and shows post-compaction ordering that retains recent user messages after the system prompt. Compacting away the active prompt lets the summarizer paraphrase or omit the instruction that the next route-target turn is supposed to answer.
Suggested fix: Account for system-prompt tokens in the compaction trigger and post-compaction fit calculation without letting them reduce the preserved recent-conversation budget to zero. Preserve at least the last real user message/current prompt verbatim, or add cut-point logic that excludes prefix context from the keep budget while still enforcing a bounded final request size.
acceptance: With a large system prompt and a small KeepRecentTokens budget, compaction still triggers when required but the next upstream-service call retains the active user prompt as a verbatim user message; add a regression test that fails if the final upstream-service request contains only system prompt plus summary after compaction.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: correctness: classify zero-cost sessions as known in process metrics
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:1083
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `sessionCostState` and `sessionDerivedCost` treat any non-positive `cost_usd` as not-known by checking `sess.CostUSD > 0`. SD-016 explicitly says known-zero values are valid and must remain `cost_state=known`; only the `-1` sentinel means unknown. Repro: from `cli/`, a temporary fixture with one correlated session using route-target `qwen/qwen3-coder-30b` and `cost_usd=0` prints `state=estimated cost=0` from `processmetrics.New(dir).Cost(...)` instead of `state=known cost=0`.
Suggested fix: Treat `cost_usd >= 0` as known, handle the `-1` sentinel explicitly, and add regression coverage for zero-cost local sessions in both cost rows and summary counters.
acceptance: A session row with cost_usd=0 is reported with cost_state=known, contributes to known-cost counts instead of estimated-cost counts, the -1 sentinel still maps to unknown, and regression tests cover zero-cost + sentinel cases

title: integration: closing_commit_sha points ddx-1210706d at a mixed commit
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 9653820 rewrites ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to e51936a9300b162ab655ffe6c1150d6ba5de06c4, but that target commit contains unrelated agent/runtime changes in cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go, cli/internal/agent/types.go, and tests in addition to the tracker-governance work. FEAT-019 uses closing_commit_sha as replay provenance and compares against that commit's diff, so this bead now points replay/evidence at a mixed implementation boundary rather than just the work governed by ddx-1210706d.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Phase 5f: rewrite observability_workdir.go to read files directly; eliminate internal Runner usage
description:
Surfaced by ddx-bc1e99ec (commit 280f58e0). The observability_workdir.go wrappers currently build a minimal Runner via NewRunner(cfg) and call its observability methods (ProbeRunnerState, LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). This keeps Runner alive as an internal implementation detail.

Goal: lift the file-reading logic OUT of Runner methods into free functions (or just inline into observability_workdir.go). Then Runner can finally die, and the 5 file-delete beads cascade.

Key insight: the observability data DDx surfaces lives in DDx-managed paths (session JSONLs in cfg.SessionLogDir, quota caches in agent module - need to call into agent for quota refresh). These are file-read operations, not Runner-state operations.

Files involved:
- cli/internal/agent/observability_workdir.go — currently uses Runner-build pattern; rewrite to direct file reads
- cli/internal/agent/runner.go — extract file-reading logic from methods then delete those methods (Runner.ProbeRunnerState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). The methods themselves are likely just thin wrappers around Runner.Config + free helpers — extract the helpers, drop the methods.
- For tmux quota refresh: call agent module helpers directly (internal/runneres/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go via the existing tmux probe ENTRY POINTS; the agent has these functions exported, e.g. ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux from internal/runneres/claude — check)

After this lands:
- Runner public methods all gone
- Runner struct can delete (Phase 5g — separate bead OR fold here)
- The 5 file-delete beads (ddx-f0cab7b3 state+registry, ddx-ea7f041c routing.go, ddx-70c48576 routing observability, ddx-1bf6c0df tier_escalation, ddx-2912e7cd discovery+deadline+probe) cascade cleanly
acceptance:
- [ ] observability_workdir.go does not build a Runner internally
- [ ] Runner.ProbeRunnerState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux methods deleted
- [ ] Runner struct fields (Registry, Discovery, Catalog, AgentUpstream-service, etc.) — only those needed by REMAINING test scaffolding kept; rest deleted
- [ ] Build clean; existing tests pass
- [ ] After this lands, the 5 file-delete beads execute cleanly

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Dashboard UI: live workers page with per-worker prompt, log, and utilization detail
description:
The server knows everything about running workers (state, runner, route-target, bead, attempts, prompt file, structured event log, token/cost/duration) via /api/agent/workers endpoints but the web UI has no live workers view. When an operator selects the eitri node they should land on (or reach in one click) an Agents page that lists every running worker from /api/agent/workers with bead-id, runner, route-target, state, elapsed, and latest status line. Clicking a row should open a worker detail page showing: (1) the rendered bead prompt from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/prompt.md, (2) the structured session log stream from .ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-<session>.jsonl rendered via FormatSessionLogLines (so tool calls, turn counts, and timings are readable), (3) a session utilization panel with input/output/total tokens, cost-if-available, duration, attempts and successes/failures from the worker record, and (4) the base-rev and result-rev link once the worker has made progress. Polling at ~2s is fine for v1; no WebSocket required.

Backing endpoints that already exist:
- GET /api/agent/workers -> list
- GET /api/agent/workers/:id -> detail
- GET /api/agent/workers/:id/log -> stdout+stderr streams

Missing backing endpoints (if not already there): GET /api/agent/workers/:id/prompt to serve prompt.md content without requiring the UI to know the execution bundle path; optionally /api/agent/workers/:id/events to serve the structured JSONL stream if /log doesn't already cover it.
acceptance: Web UI route /nodes/:nodeId/agents (or /workers) lists all workers from /api/agent/workers with auto-refresh every ~2s. A worker row links to /nodes/:nodeId/agents/:workerId with three panels: prompt, log stream (structured events rendered with FormatSessionLogLines symbols), utilization (tokens/cost/duration/attempts). Both pages work against the alpha14 server without any additional backend work beyond optionally exposing the prompt content. Playwright test covers: list-shows-running-worker, list-refreshes-on-state-change, detail-renders-prompt-log-and-utilization.

title: claude profile routing must dispatch executable family aliases
description: Profile routing can resolve Claude family aliases to versioned route-target IDs such as opus-4.7 or stale claude-opus-4-6. Current Claude Code accepts the generic family alias (for example, opus) while rejecting some versioned IDs, so DDx smart routing can fail before an agent run starts.
acceptance: 1. Claude subprocess execution normalizes family aliases and versioned family pins to CLI-executable family aliases before dispatch. 2. Tests cover generic sonnet, opus-4.7, and claude-opus-4-6 normalization. 3. go test ./... passes. 4. A DDx build using the patched agent alpha can run ddx agent run --profile smart --text 'Respond with exactly: ok' successfully.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Add route-target-route config schema and backend compatibility translation
description: Implement the config-layer replacement described in FEAT-004, SD-005, and plan-2026-04-10-route-target-first-routing.md. Add routing/route-target_routes config types keyed by requested route-target or canonical target, plus candidate records with upstream-service, optional concrete route-target override, and priority. Preserve direct named-upstream-service config. Load legacy backends/default_backend as deprecated compatibility input, translate them into internal route-target-route records at config-load time, and emit a deprecation warning path that the CLI can surface without breaking existing users. Validation should fail early for unknown upstream-services, empty candidate lists, and unknown route names before agent.Run() starts. Background: DDx expects to hand the embedded runner only route-target intent, not backend labels, so the config surface must make route-target routes the canonical inner-routing abstraction while remaining backward compatible for one migration window.
acceptance: Config supports routing/route-target_routes plus deprecated backend translation; invalid routes fail during config resolution; legacy backend config maps to the same upstream-service choice as equivalent route-target-route config with a visible deprecation warning path; unit tests cover route parsing, translation, validation, and default-route-target/default-upstream-service precedence.

title: task: implement FEAT-018 package manifest and plugin doctor validation
description:
<context-digest>
Review area: plugin API implementation. Evidence covers FEAT-018, SD-018, BuiltinRegistry, and the missing ddx doctor --plugins validation path.
</context-digest>
Discovered from review issue hx-97fb0ffc during the 2026-04-09 repo alignment reconciliation.

Current drift:
- FEAT-018 and SD-018 define `package.yaml`, `api_version`, and shared install/doctor validation.
- Install and search paths still depend on BuiltinRegistry() as the only authoritative package descriptor source.
- `ddx doctor` has no `--plugins` audit surface for missing manifests, missing SKILL.md files, broken symlinks, or unsupported schema fields.

Goal: make the documented plugin contract executable at install time and auditable through doctor, with regression coverage.
acceptance: DDx reads package.yaml manifests with api_version compatibility checks during install or local package validation; ddx doctor --plugins reports missing manifests, missing SKILL.md, broken symlinks, and required-field or schema problems; shared validation logic is used by both install and doctor paths; and regression tests cover manifest parsing plus plugin-audit failure modes

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing
description:
## Motivation

DDx consumer goal G3: "the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**." Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.

## Scope

### Data route-target

Add to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:

- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.
- `CostSource string` — `"catalog" | "subscription" | "unknown" | "user-config"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.

Populate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:

- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: "unknown"`.
- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:
  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).
  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).
  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).
  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.
  - `CostSource: "subscription"`.
- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: "unknown"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.

### Scoring changes

`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:
1. Eligible
2. Quota OK / healthy
3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged
4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new
5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)
6. Name

For unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: "unknown"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.

### Unknown-cost policy

A documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: "unknown"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.

## Subscription-quota pricing curve

Default curve (tunable):
- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0
- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target
- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token
- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)
acceptance:
1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.
3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.
4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).
5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.
6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: "unknown"`.
7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.
8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.
9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).
10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.

bead.title: Cost-aware routing: CostUSDPer1kTokens on Upstream-serviceEntry + cost tiebreak + unknown-cost + subscription pricing
bead.description:
## Motivation

DDx consumer goal G3: "the selected upstream-service should be the 'best' that meets whatever the requirements are — available, with quota available, **and lowest cost**." Today the routing engine has no cost dimension on `Upstream-serviceEntry`; scoring uses only quota + cooldown + perf + locality. When two upstream-services serve the same route-target with different prices, the engine picks by name or score, not cost.

## Scope

### Data route-target

Add to `internal/routing.Upstream-serviceEntry`:

- `CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` — nil/zero if unknown.
- `CostSource string` — `"catalog" | "subscription" | "unknown" | "user-config"`. Surfaces in decision trace so operators know where the number came from.

Populate at `buildRoutingInputs` time:

- **Endpoint upstream-services (openrouter, lmstudio, omlx, ollama, minimax, qwen, zai, openai):** look up in the existing route-target catalog (`internal/route-targetcatalog`) by resolved route-target ID. When the catalog has a price, use it. When absent, leave zero + `CostSource: "unknown"`.
- **Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini):** compute an **effective** cost as a function of quota utilization:
  - At 0-70% quota used: effective cost = 0 (fixed-cost subscription, near-zero marginal).
  - At 70-90%: effective cost = low (encourage spreading load).
  - At 90-100%: effective cost = high (near-quota demotion).
  - Exact numbers in the bead acceptance; tunable via `ServiceOptions`.
  - `CostSource: "subscription"`.
- **`agent` runner (local via endpoints):** cost = electricity estimate from config (off by default — `CostSource: "unknown"` when not configured). Operators who want to route-target local cost set `ServiceOptions.LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens`.

### Scoring changes

`internal/routing/score.go`: add cost as a tiebreak dimension **after** eligibility, quota OK, and base score. Order becomes:
1. Eligible
2. Quota OK / healthy
3. Base score (profile policy, cooldowns, perf bias) — unchanged
4. **Cost (lower is better)** — new
5. Locality (local cost-class preferred)
6. Name

For unknown-cost upstream-services (`CostSource: "unknown"`): treat as **neutral** in the cost tiebreak — neither preferred nor demoted, so they don't starve known-cost upstream-services but aren't penalized into last place.

### Unknown-cost policy

A documented rule: when all viable candidates have `CostSource: "unknown"`, the cost tiebreak is skipped and locality/name decide. Test covers this.

## Subscription-quota pricing curve

Default curve (tunable):
- 0-70% used: effective $/1k tokens = 0
- 70-80%: 0.1× the pay-per-token rate for the equivalent route-target
- 80-90%: 0.3× pay-per-token
- 90-100%: 1.2× pay-per-token (demote below pay-per-token so routing drains the subscription gracefully)
bead.acceptance:
1. `Upstream-serviceEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokens float64` and `CostSource string` fields added with godoc.
2. `buildRoutingInputs` populates both fields for endpoint upstream-services via route-target catalog lookup. Test seeds a catalog with 3 upstream-services at different prices for the same route-target and asserts correct population.
3. Subscription runneres (claude, codex, gemini) compute effective cost as a function of quota utilization per the curve in description. Unit test for each runner + each curve band.
4. Scoring tiebreak applies cost after base score, before locality. Test: 2 upstream-services with identical score, different cost → lower cost wins. 2 upstream-services with identical score, identical cost → locality decides (unchanged).
5. Unknown-cost upstream-services are neutral in the tiebreak: test with mixed known and unknown costs asserts known-cost upstream-services are not starved, unknown-cost upstream-services are not demoted into last place.
6. `LocalCostUSDPer1kTokens` in `ServiceOptions` is honored when set; agent runner with local endpoints uses it. Unset = `CostSource: "unknown"`.
7. Near-quota demotion actually works: seed a claude runner at 92% quota, seed an openrouter upstream-service with equivalent route-target at known cost → openrouter wins.
8. Cost and CostSource appear in `RouteCandidate` (via agent-53f38d95) so the decision trace shows which upstream-service was picked and why.
9. Migration: existing tests pass unchanged (all current scoring cases assume zero-cost, which should still work).
10. Depends on agent-53f38d95 (decision trace) landing first so `CostSource` surfacing has a home.

title: chore(graphql): wire WatcherHub.Close on server shutdown + dedupe subscription test helpers
description:
Two minor cleanups from opus review of Stage 2.11 and 2.12:

1. bead.WatcherHub goroutines leak on server shutdown. Server.New at cli/internal/server/server.go:63 creates s.beadHub via bead.NewWatcherHub, but Server has no Shutdown/Close method that invokes s.beadHub.Close(). On process exit the goroutines terminate with the process, but on graceful Shutdown (never implemented today, but will be needed for multi-test scenarios and systemd restart with drain) the watchers leak. Add a Server.Shutdown method and invoke s.beadHub.Close() + coordinatorRegistry.StopAll() from it.

2. Subscription test helpers duplicate ~90 lines of graphql-transport-ws WebSocket setup across 3 test files: newGraphQLHandlerWithBeadBus, newGraphQLHandlerWithExecLogs, newGraphQLHandlerWithCoordMetrics. Each does the same httptest.NewServer + gqlgen config + transport registration. Extract to a single test helper newGraphQLSubscriptionTestServer(resolver *Resolver) or equivalent that takes a fully-populated resolver and returns the server + dial function. Cosmetic cleanup, makes adding Stage 3+ subscription tests cheap.

Not blocking anything. File under the migration epic.
acceptance: (1) Server.Shutdown method exists, calls s.beadHub.Close() and coordinatorRegistry.StopAll() in sequence, returns any first error. (2) At least one test calls Server.Shutdown and verifies both are invoked (e.g. via goroutine count or channel close). (3) Subscription test setup is extracted to a single helper function used by all 3 existing tests. (4) go test ./internal/server/... -count=1 passes.

title: Tighten cmd/agent boundary enforcement and CI checks
description:
The existing boundary tests only ban agentcore.Run in cmd/agent/main.go and require DecodeServiceEvent on the execute path. They do not prevent the broader leak: the allowlist in cmd/agent/service_boundary_test.go still permits imports of internal/core, internal/session, internal/tool, internal/upstream-service/openai, and other packages that should be forbidden once the service boundary is real. I confirmed that the current boundary suite passes while these leaks remain: go test ./cmd/agent -run "TestCLI(ServiceContractUsesTypedEventDecoder|MainDoesNotImportOrCallInternalCoreRun|InternalImportBoundaryAllowlist)" returns ok.

Tighten the executable boundary spec so regressions fail immediately. Replace the permissive allowlist with the minimal set that truly has no public replacement, add negative checks for forbidden internal packages and symbols, and wire the check into the normal test path/CI gate used for cmd/agent. If any CLI-visible behavior still needs a forbidden internal package, that is a blocker to fix in code or by extending the public contract first.

In-scope files:
- cmd/agent/service_boundary_test.go
- any helper test files or CI/test scripts needed to enforce the boundary
- contributor guidance if it needs to point at the executable boundary spec

Out of scope:
- The actual routing/session-log/upstream-service refactors except as needed to make the stricter boundary tests pass
acceptance:
1. Boundary tests fail on forbidden cmd/agent dependencies such as internal/core, internal/upstream-service/*, internal/session, internal/tool, internal/compaction, internal/runneres, and internal/routing.
2. The stricter boundary test suite is part of the normal cmd/agent test path or CI gate.
3. Verification passes: go test ./cmd/agent -run "TestCLI(ServiceContractUsesTypedEventDecoder|MainDoesNotImportOrCallInternalCoreRun|InternalImportBoundaryAllowlist|.*Boundary.*)"

title: upstream-services: classify verified reasoning_control and demote lmstudio
description:
Add an explicit upstream-service capability signal for verified reasoning control and classify LM Studio OpenAI-compatible as not having it. OMLX should advertise `reasoning_control`; LM Studio should not. LM Studio OpenAI-compatible must stop claiming request-level reasoning support in upstream-service capability declarations so routing and tests do not treat it as equivalent to OMLX. rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Enforce stable prefix ordering in prompt construction
description:
Cache efficiency requires that stable content (system prompt, persona, tool definitions, file context) comes before variable content (user message, current task). Any change to early tokens invalidates all downstream cache entries.

Audit prompt construction in ddx-agent and enforce ordering rules:
1. System prompt / persona — must be deterministic, no dynamic fields (timestamps, file lists, git hashes in this block)
2. Tool definitions — stable, comes after system prompt
3. Persistent file context — ordered consistently (e.g. alphabetically) within a session
4. Conversation history — append-only, never reorder
5. Current user turn — always last

Add a test that verifies the same session produces byte-identical prefix across multiple calls.
acceptance: Prompt construction test confirms stable prefix is byte-identical across repeated calls with same context; no timestamps or dynamic fields in system prompt block

description: opencode is a registered supported subprocess runner but should remain explicit-only until it has full baseline coverage comparable to primary runneres. Add or audit coverage for argument construction, prompt/workdir delivery, route-target selection/listing, reasoning/variant support, token usage, error/cancel behavior, permission semantics, final metadata, and live or recorded evidence. Only promote opencode to auto-routing eligibility after the evidence exists.\n\nIn-scope files are expected to include internal/runneres/opencode/, service/routing tests, capability metadata, and docs evidence.
acceptance:
1. opencode runner tests cover route-target flag, workdir, prompt delivery, timeout/cancellation cleanup, stderr/exit mapping, final text, and usage parsing if supported.
2. Route-target listing and reasoning/variant support are implemented with source metadata or explicitly declared unsupported with routing tests.
3. Permission semantics are documented and tested.
4. Capability matrix/docs include evidence pointers and blockers.
5. opencode is promoted to auto-routing eligibility only if AC1-AC4 are satisfied; otherwise it remains explicit-only.
6. go test ./... passes.

rationale: closed with commit evidence and no decomposition signal
title: Add project-scoped MCP tools: ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware tool selection
description:
## Scope
Add project-awareness to the MCP tool layer per SD-019 requirements.

## Context
FEAT-002/SD-019 requires ddx_list_projects, ddx_show_project, and project-aware selection semantics for all existing project-local MCP tools. Currently, all 18 MCP tools operate against the single working directory without project scoping.

## Implementation approach
1. Add ddx_list_projects tool — returns project registry entries
2. Add ddx_show_project tool — returns single project detail by ID
3. Add optional "project" parameter to all project-local tools
4. When project param is provided, resolve via GetProjectByID and use that project's path
5. When omitted and exactly one project registered, auto-resolve (singleton compat)
6. When omitted and >1 project registered, return error requesting disambiguation
7. Add MCP-specific tests

## Depends on
ddx-36d2775f (route middleware — shares projectResolver logic)

## Files to touch
- cli/internal/server/mcp.go (or mcp_handler.go) — tool registration + project param
- cli/internal/server/server_test.go or mcp_test.go — MCP project tests
acceptance: (1) MCP tools ddx_list_projects and ddx_show_project are registered and functional. (2) Existing MCP tools that read project-local data (ddx_list_documents, ddx_read_document, ddx_search, ddx_list_beads, ddx_show_bead, ddx_doc_graph, etc.) accept an optional project parameter for multi-project disambiguation. (3) When exactly one project is registered, tools work without project parameter (singleton compat). (4) cd cli && go test ./internal/server -run 'TestMCP' -count=1 passes with at least one test covering project-aware MCP tool selection.

title: correctness: reject malformed prompt envelopes missing id
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: cmd/ddx-agent/main.go:331
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new probe heuristic only treats JSON as a DDx prompt envelope when `kind` is paired with `id`, `inputs`, `response_schema`, or `callback`. A malformed envelope like {"kind":"prompt","prompt":"Read main.go"} is therefore treated as raw prompt text instead of failing validation, so execution proceeds to upstream-service selection. This violates the structured-I/O contract in the PRD and leaves invalid envelope input non-deterministic. Repro: `go run ./cmd/ddx-agent -p '{"kind":"prompt","prompt":"Read main.go"}' -upstream-service nonexistent` returns `config: unknown upstream-service "nonexistent"` instead of `invalid prompt envelope`.\nSuggested fix: Treat objects that contain `kind` plus `prompt` as envelope candidates too, so missing required `id` still reaches envelope validation; add regression coverage for missing-id envelopes across inline, stdin, and @file inputs.
acceptance: A JSON payload intended as a DDx prompt envelope but missing the required id field fails with an invalid prompt envelope error for -p, stdin, and @file input paths, while non-envelope JSON objects still pass through as raw prompt text; regression tests cover both behaviors.

rationale: closed focused bead with small acceptance surface
title: Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default
description:
<context-digest>
Alpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.
</context-digest>

`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.
acceptance: `ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.

## Why this matters

bead.acceptance: `ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.

title: correctness: fix hx-72b9a368 closing commit provenance
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:28
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 2b3e824 closes hx-72b9a368 after updating SD-016, but the bead row now records closing_commit_sha=34b87b97d99762e16dfcea49044ea68457b4c5c6, which is the previous metadata-only commit about ddx-1210706d provenance rather than the commit that actually lands the SD-016 cost-sentinel fix. FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary and checks out its parent before comparing against that commit's diff, so replaying hx-72b9a368 would now diff the wrong change and lose traceability to the documentation fix the bead actually governed.
Suggested fix: Make the close path record the commit that actually lands the bead closure, then repair hx-72b9a368 so its closing provenance is 2b3e824d812ac23d2d3ac588da00d57cfda01601 or another explicitly documented and tested value with unambiguous replay semantics.
acceptance: ddx bead show hx-72b9a368 --json either omits closing_commit_sha or reports 2b3e824d812ac23d2d3ac588da00d57cfda01601, and ddx agent replay hx-72b9a368 uses the SD-016 sentinel-fix commit boundary rather than the unrelated 34b87b9 tracker-metadata diff

title: Update DDx to ddx-agent v0.1.1
description:
Upstream `DocumentDrivenDX/agent` now has point release `v0.1.1`. Update DDx to consume that release and reconcile any integration assumptions that changed since `v0.1.0`.

Key upstream changes since `v0.1.0`:
- OTel telemetry landed for session, chat-attempt, and tool spans/metrics, with a documented telemetry contract and runtime-specific cost attribution.
- Upstream-service retries moved into the runtime so upstream-services execute one attempt per call and expose richer attempt metadata.
- Session continuity/history support landed in `Run`.
- `ddx-agent usage` reporting landed.
- Tool path handling now resolves symlink chains.
- Compaction behavior was hardened: prefix-aware accounting, balanced compaction events, and fail-closed handling when compaction cannot fit within budget.
- Self-update cache writing was fixed for first-run homes without an existing cache directory.
- Mixed-provenance known-cost session rollups now preserve the root known cost instead of downgrading to unknown.

DDx should review whether any wrapper logic, telemetry ingestion, usage/reporting integration, or runtime assumptions need adjustment when moving from `v0.1.0` to `v0.1.1`.
acceptance: DDx updates its ddx-agent dependency/reference to upstream v0.1.1; any required integration or config changes for retry ownership, telemetry, usage/session continuity, or tool path behavior are implemented; affected tests pass; and any noteworthy compatibility or rollout notes are recorded in the bead or linked docs

title: Expand route-target-backed integration coverage for the shipped tool surface
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Route-target-backed acceptance should validate the benchmark-oriented tool surface, not just unit APIs · Environment-gated tests must skip cleanly with an explicit reason</principles>
<concerns>real upstream-service/tool interaction | benchmark navigation tools | integration build tags</concerns>
<practices>Extend build-tagged integration tests instead of adding flaky default-suite coverage · Prefer compact scenarios that exercise multiple tools in one run</practices>
<adrs>None beyond feature-level contracts</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-002 AC-FEAT-002-06</governing>
</context-digest>

The current integration suite proves simple read/edit flows against LM Studio, but it does not exercise the broader shipped tool surface that FEAT-002 now claims. Add at least one environment-gated route-target-backed acceptance scenario that drives a real upstream-service through navigation and editing work using the benchmark-oriented tools (`glob`, `grep`, `ls`, `patch`, and/or `task`) rather than only `read`/`edit`.

The test should:
- create a disposable workspace with a small but non-trivial file graph
- prompt the route-target to discover a target via navigation tools, make a change, and confirm the result
- assert on actual tool-call logs and resulting files, not just the final assistant text
- skip explicitly when the configured integration environment is unavailable

Relevant files: `integration_test.go`, `tool/`, and any upstream-service-specific integration helpers.
acceptance: An environment-gated integration test covers AC-FEAT-002-06 by exercising the benchmark-oriented tool surface against a real upstream-service, asserting on tool-call logs and workspace mutations; the integration suite passes when a backend is configured and otherwise skips explicitly.

title: review: collapse cmd/bead_review.go into agent.BuildReviewPrompt
description:
Stage B of FEAT-022. Collapse the duplicate review-prompt assembler in
cli/cmd/bead_review.go into agent.BuildReviewPrompt. A byte-equivalence
golden fixture, CAPTURED BEFORE THE DUPLICATE IS DELETED, gates the
removal.

Implements FEAT-022 requirement §4. Satisfies US-227.

Depends on Stage A1 (ddx-64299657) — evidence primitives must exist so
the golden-fixture test can import them cleanly (though this bead does
NOT yet change behavior via caps — that's Stage C1).

Workflow (non-negotiable, prevents half-done landings):

1. BEFORE touching any code, run the current (pre-collapse) CLI review
   path against a committed fixture bead/rev/refs and write the
   resulting prompt bytes to a golden file at
   cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/collapse_golden.txt. Commit that
   golden as the first commit on this bead's branch. The fixture MUST
   include `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"`, and `'` characters in bead notes and
   governing-doc content so the escape-semantics divergence
   (xml.EscapeText in cmd vs NewReplacer in agent) is forced to
   surface.

2. Now collapse: delete cli/cmd/bead_review.go's buildBeadReviewPrompt
   (at :150), beadReviewInstructions const (at :43), and
   beadReviewXMLEscape (at :240). Rewire the CLI handler to call
   agent.BuildReviewPrompt directly.

3. If escape semantics differ between old and new on the committed
   fixture: converge on agent.reviewXMLEscape's behavior unless a
   specific failure mode requires the CLI's quote-escape. In that
   case extend BuildReviewPrompt with a typed flag parameter (e.g.
   EscapeQuotes bool). Do NOT keep a duplicate escape helper.

4. The post-collapse CLI review path MUST produce bytes identical to
   the golden file when run against the same fixture inputs.

In-scope files:
- cli/cmd/bead_review.go (deletions + rewire to agent package)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (only if a typed flag is
  needed per step 3; document and justify any addition)
- cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/ (new golden fixture)
- cli/cmd/bead_review_test.go (new): golden-file diff test +
  CLI-handler wiring test

Out-of-scope (do NOT touch):
- cli/cmd/bead_review.go's beadReviewGitShow helper at :164 — this is
  git invocation, not prompt assembly. KEEP IT.
- Adding any cap / evidence-primitive usage to BuildReviewPrompt's
  assembly logic — that's Stage C1. This bead is pure deduplication.

Rollback: revert the deletions, restore duplicate symbols. Golden
fixture can be retained regardless.
acceptance: rg 'buildBeadReviewPrompt|beadReviewInstructions|beadReviewXMLEscape' cli/cmd/bead_review.go exits 1 (zero matches). cli/cmd/testdata/bead_review/collapse_golden.txt exists and contains XML-escape-sensitive characters (<, >, &, double-quote, single-quote). cd cli && go test -run TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden ./cmd/... passes: the test invokes the post-collapse CLI review path against the fixture and diffs output against collapse_golden.txt. cd cli && go test ./... passes. cd cli && go vet ./... passes. grep -c 'beadReviewGitShow' cli/cmd/bead_review.go returns a positive integer (helper retained). Commits on this bead's branch include one that adds collapse_golden.txt BEFORE any commit that deletes the duplicate symbols (verifiable via git log --diff-filter=D of the duplicate-symbol lines vs git log --diff-filter=A of the golden file).
